item.title: 基于本体知识的时序知识图谱外推方法
item.content:encoded:
利用本体知识提升时序知识图谱外推性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为OntoTKGE的框架，通过整合本体知识与时间知识，提升时序知识图谱（TKG）外推模型的性能。该方法利用本体知识中的概念层级关系，帮助实体继承其他同类实体的行为模式，从而缓解实体历史交互数据稀疏的问题。实验表明，OntoTKGE在多个数据集上显著提升了现有模型的性能，并优于多项先进方法。

item.title: 基于CVA架构的大型语言模型行为优化研究
item.content:encoded:
提升LLM行为真实性，缓解价值极化问题。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在模拟人类行为时存在的行为僵化问题，并指出当前评估方法存在自我参照偏差。通过与真实数据对比，发现增强提示驱动推理反而加剧价值极化，导致群体多样性下降。为此，作者提出基于S-O-R模型和人类价值观理论的Context-Value-Action（CVA）架构，通过独立的价值验证器实现行为生成与认知推理的解耦，有效提升行为真实性与可解释性。实验结果表明，CVA在包含110万真实交互记录的CVABench数据集上优于现有基线方法。

item.title: AI机器身份治理的缺失与解决方案
item.content:encoded:
AI机器身份缺乏治理框架，导致重大安全风险。



摘要： 文章指出，AI系统使用的机器身份（如AI代理、API令牌等）在企业环境中数量远超人类身份，但目前缺乏统一的治理框架。这导致了严重的安全风险，例如2024年CrowdStrike事件中因未受监管的自动化代理造成54亿至100亿美元损失。文章提出四个原创贡献：AI-Identity Risk Taxonomy（AIRT）用于分类37种风险；Machine Identity Governance Taxonomy（MIGT）作为整合治理框架；针对国家行为者的威胁模型；以及跨司法管辖区的监管协调结构。最后，还提供了一个四阶段实施路线图，以推动企业实际应用。

item.title: Market-Bench：评估大语言模型经济能力的基准测试
item.content:encoded:
评估大语言模型在经济任务中的表现，揭示其资源管理能力差异。



摘要： 本文提出 Market-Bench，一个用于评估大语言模型（LLMs）在经济相关任务中表现的基准测试。通过构建多智能体供应链经济模型，LLMs 作为零售商代理参与采购和零售过程。在采购阶段，LLMs 在预算受限的拍卖中竞标库存；在零售阶段，LLMs 设定价格、生成营销标语，并通过角色注意力机制向买家提供。该基准记录完整的出价、价格、标语、销售和财务状态轨迹，支持经济、运营和语义指标的自动评估。对 20 个开源与闭源 LLM 代理的基准测试显示显著性能差异，仅少数模型能持续实现资本增值，多数则接近盈亏平衡点。

item.title: PRepair：减少过度修改的精准代码修复框架
item.content:encoded:
PRepair通过最小化修改提升代码修复精度。



摘要： 本文提出PRepair，一种减少过度修改的代码修复框架。该框架包含Self-Breaking和Self-Repairing两个组件，通过控制性注入错误和编辑感知奖励机制，提高修复准确率。实验表明，PRepair在fix_1@1指标上提升31.4%，并提升解码吞吐量，具有实际应用潜力。

item.title: 基于强化学习与大语言模型的交通信号控制框架CuraLight
item.content:encoded:
CuraLight提升交通信号控制效率，减少通行时间与排队长度。



摘要： 本文提出CuraLight，一个以大语言模型为中心的交通信号控制框架，结合强化学习进行环境探索并生成高质量交互轨迹，用于优化模型训练。通过多大语言模型的结构化辩论系统，进一步提升信号控制决策的准确性。实验在SUMO平台上进行，覆盖多个真实城市网络，结果表明CuraLight在平均通行时间、队列长度和等待时间方面均优于现有方法，显示出其在可扩展性和可解释性方面的优势。

item.title: 多轮对话中的语言模型校准方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究多轮对话中模型置信度校准，提升交互可靠性。



摘要： 本文提出多轮校准任务，将模型置信度校准从静态属性转化为动态挑战。通过引入ECE@T指标评估多轮校准效果，发现用户反馈可能降低校准性能。为此，作者提出MTCal方法优化校准，并结合ConfChat策略提升多轮对话的准确性和一致性。实验表明，该方法在多轮场景中表现优异，有助于实现更安全、可靠的大型语言模型应用。

item.title: Character Error Vector：一种新的OCR评估方法
item.content:encoded:
提出CEV作为OCR质量评估的新方法，优于传统CER。



摘要： 本文介绍了Character Error Vector (CEV)，一种用于评估OCR质量的袋式字符评估方法。与传统的CER不同，CEV可以分解为解析、OCR和交互错误部分，从而帮助从业者更精准地定位影响文本提取质量的关键环节。研究展示了两种实现方式：SpACER和基于Jensen-Shannon距离的字符分布方法。通过与CER等其他指标的对比验证，CEV在页面级OCR评估中表现出色，尤其在处理复杂布局的档案报纸数据时，传统流程方法优于当前最先进的端到端模型。CEV需要字符级位置信息以达到最佳效果，但使用易获取的阈值即可预测主要错误来源，F1值达0.91。研究还提供了Python库以支持文档理解研究。

item.title: Paper Circle：基于多智能体的学术文献发现与分析系统
item.content:encoded:
Paper Circle 提供高效学术文献发现与分析工具。



摘要： 本文介绍了 Paper Circle，一个基于多智能体大型语言模型的学术文献发现与分析系统。该系统包含两个互补流程：发现流程整合了多源检索、多标准评分、多样性感知排序和结构化输出；分析流程将单篇论文转化为包含概念、方法、实验等类型节点的知识图谱，支持图感知问答和覆盖验证。系统在代码器 LLM 基础上实现，生成可复现的 JSON、CSV、BibTeX 等格式输出。文章描述了系统架构、代理角色、检索与评分方法、知识图谱模式及评估接口，并在论文检索与综述生成任务中进行了基准测试。

item.title: Action Images：基于可解释动作图像的统一世界动作模型
item.content:encoded:
提出Action Images，实现零样本策略学习与视频生成。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Action Images的统一世界动作模型，将机器人控制转化为多视角视频生成任务。通过将7自由度机器人动作转化为基于像素的可解释动作图像，该模型无需单独策略头即可直接利用视频骨干网络进行策略学习。该方法不仅支持零样本策略执行，还能够进行视频-动作联合生成、动作条件视频生成和动作标注。实验表明，该模型在RLBench和真实世界任务中取得了最佳的零样本成功率，并提升了视频-动作联合生成的质量，验证了可解释动作图像在策略学习中的有效性。

item.title: 多步预测提升语言模型内部世界建模能力
item.content:encoded:
多步预测促进语言模型学习更结构化的表示，但存在结构幻觉问题。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型是否能够发展出连贯的内部世界模型，并分析了多步预测（MTP）在学习结构化表示方面的潜力。研究指出，MTP通过梯度耦合诱导表征收缩性，有助于模型收敛到内部信念状态，但标准MTP常出现结构幻觉，即离散标记监督导致潜在空间中违反环境约束的捷径。为此，作者提出LSE-MTP方法，通过锚定真实隐藏状态轨迹来改善预测。实验表明，该方法在合成图和曼哈顿出租车数据上有效提升了表征对齐度，减少了结构幻觉并增强了鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于外观分解的多遍历场景重建方法ADM-GS
item.content:encoded:
提出ADM-GS方法解决多遍历场景重建中的外观不一致问题。



摘要： 本文提出ADM-GS（Appearance Decomposition Gaussian Splatting for Multi-Traversal Reconstruction），一种用于多遍历场景重建的框架。该方法通过显式分解静态背景的外观，将外观分为与遍历无关的材质属性和与遍历相关的光照变化，以缓解不同遍历间的外观混淆。作者设计了一种神经光场，采用频率分离的混合编码策略，分别捕捉低频漫反射光照和高频镜面反射。在Argoverse 2和Waymo Open数据集上的定量评估表明，ADM-GS在多遍历实验中相比现有基线方法提升了0.98 dB的PSNR，并实现了更一致的外观表现。代码将在GitHub上公开。

item.content:encoded:
SBQE提升量子机器学习效率，无需编码门即可实现高准确率。



摘要： 本文提出Shot-Based Quantum Encoding (SBQE) 方法，解决量子机器学习中数据加载效率低的问题。该方法通过将硬件资源shots按数据依赖的分布分配到多个初始量子态，构建混合态表示，其期望值与经典概率线性相关，可与非线性激活函数结合。实验表明，SBQE在Fashion MNIST和Semeion数据集上分别达到80.95%和89.1%的测试准确率，优于传统编码方式且无需数据编码门。

item.title: 基于神经符号方法的采购文件验证系统
item.content:encoded:
神经符号方法提升采购文件验证的可解释性与合规性。



摘要： 本文提出一种结合符号与非符号人工智能的神经符号方法，用于监管公共机构中采购文件的验证。通过语言模型提取信息，并利用逻辑张量网络进行可审计决策。该方法将领域知识与语言模型的语义理解相结合，使决策可通过谓词值、规则真值和对应文本片段进行解释，从而实现基于真实采购文档语料的规则检查。实验表明，该方法性能与现有模型相当，但具有更高的可解释性、模块化谓词提取和对可解释AI的支持。

item.title: 基于Σ-Voxfield的可扩展户外驾驶场景生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种3D生成框架，支持大规模户外场景生成与多视角渲染。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于Σ-Voxfield网格的3D生成框架，通过在每个占用体素中存储固定数量的着色表面样本，实现多视角一致的大规模户外场景生成。该方法利用语义条件扩散模型，在局部体素邻域内操作，并结合3D位置编码捕捉空间结构。通过渐进式空间扩展技术，实现对大范围区域的生成。最终通过延迟渲染模块生成逼真图像，无需逐场景优化。实验表明，该方法能够在合理计算成本下生成多样化的城市户外场景，并支持多种传感器配置和相机轨迹。

item.title: 提升扩散语言模型推理速度的自校正方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究提出新框架提升扩散模型在少步采样中的性能。



摘要： 本文研究了连续扩散语言模型中自校正机制在少步采样时性能下降的问题，发现错误会随着去噪步骤累积，影响生成质量。为此，作者提出一种新的训练框架，通过扰动自校正信号来匹配推理噪声，增强对先前估计误差的鲁棒性。同时引入基于token的噪声感知机制，防止训练饱和，提升优化效果。实验表明该框架在条件生成任务中优于传统模型，并实现400倍的推理加速。

item.title: 探索MoE大模型中的领域专家及其应用
item.content:encoded:
研究发现MoE模型中存在领域专家，并提出DSMoE框架提升性能。



摘要： 在大型语言模型（LLM）时代，混合专家（MoE）架构被广泛用于提高计算效率。本文探讨了MoE模型中是否存在领域特定专家这一关键问题，并通过实验验证了其存在性。基于此，作者提出了Domain Steering Mixture of Experts（DSMoE）框架，在不增加推理成本的情况下，优于现有先进MoE模型和基线方法。实验结果表明，该方法在多个开源MoE模型上表现出色，具有良好的泛化能力，且无需额外训练。

item.title: 树决策图在布尔函数建模中的应用
item.content:encoded:
树决策图扩展了OBDD，具有更高效的表示和计算能力。



摘要： 文章介绍了树决策图（TDD）作为一种布尔函数的模型，它扩展了有序二叉决策图（OBDD）。TDD可以看作是结构化d-DNNF的一种限制形式，即遵循特定vtree的d-DNNF。研究表明，TDD在模型计数、枚举、条件化和应用等方面与OBDD具有相同的可处理性，并且更加简洁。特别是，文章指出，具有treewidth为k的CNF公式可以用FPT大小的TDD表示，而这是OBDD无法实现的。此外，研究还探讨了通过自底向上编译将CNF公式转换为确定性TDD的复杂性，并将其与Bova和Szeider提出的因子宽度概念相关联。

item.title: In-Place Test-Time Training：提升大语言模型持续学习能力的新框架
item.content:encoded:
In-Place TTT使大语言模型在推理时动态更新参数，提升适应新信息的能力。



摘要： 本文提出In-Place Test-Time Training（In-Place TTT），一种无需重新训练即可让大语言模型在推理时动态调整参数的框架。该方法利用MLP块的最终投影矩阵作为可变参数，实现高效、兼容的测试时训练。同时，通过针对自回归语言建模任务设计的目标函数，提升了模型在长上下文任务中的表现。实验表明，该框架在4B参数模型上表现出色，并优于现有TTT方法。研究为大语言模型的持续学习提供了新的方向。

item.title: 基于天气条件的多模态感知自适应路由3D目标检测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种自适应多模态融合方法提升恶劣天气下的3D目标检测性能。



摘要： 本文针对恶劣天气下3D目标检测的挑战，提出一种基于天气条件的多模态感知自适应路由框架。该框架通过维护三个并行的3D特征流（纯LiDAR、纯4D雷达和条件门控融合），结合轻量级路由器动态调整各模态权重，实现对环境变化的自适应响应。同时引入天气监督学习策略，防止分支坍塌并增强路由行为的多样性。实验表明，该方法在K-Radar基准上取得最佳性能，并提供可解释的模态偏好分析。

item.title: 指令调优模型的指令遵循机制研究
item.content:encoded:
指令调优模型依赖多样技能协调而非统一机制。



摘要： 本文研究了指令调优语言模型的指令遵循机制，通过九个不同任务的诊断探测发现，指令遵循并非基于统一机制，而是依赖于多样化的语言技能协调。实验表明，跨任务迁移能力较弱，且任务复杂度在模型层中呈现分层结构。此外，约束满足主要在生成过程中动态监控，而非预生成规划。这些结果表明，指令遵循更倾向于技能组合而非单一抽象过程。

item.title: 基于单点监督的红外小目标检测方法SPIRE
item.content:encoded:
提出SPIRE方法，提升红外小目标检测性能。



摘要： 本文针对红外小目标检测（IRSTD）中目标像素少、边界模糊的问题，提出一种基于单点监督的新型检测方法SPIRE。该方法将IRSTD重新定义为中心点回归任务，通过设计点响应先验监督（PRPS）和高分辨率概率编码器（HRPE），实现无需解码器的端到端回归。SPIRE有效提升了检测精度，降低了误报率和计算成本，并在多个基准数据集上验证了其优越性。

item.title: 面向长图像描述的细粒度幻觉检测基准研究
item.content:encoded:
提出DetailVerifyBench，用于评估长图像描述中的幻觉定位能力。



摘要： 本文针对多模态大语言模型生成的长图像描述中幻觉检测与定位的挑战，提出了一种名为DetailVerifyBench的新基准。该基准包含5个不同领域的1000张高质量图像，平均每段描述超过200词，并提供了密集的、基于token级别的多种幻觉类型标注，是目前最具有挑战性的长图像描述幻觉定位评估基准。该研究旨在提升图像描述系统的可靠性。

item.title: 基于广义贝叶斯推断的数据分布估值框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型数据分布估值方法，提升数据评估效率。



摘要： 本文研究数据分布估值问题，旨在通过样本量化数据分布的价值。作者提出了一种名为广义贝叶斯估值的框架，利用基于可迁移性度量构建的损失函数进行广义贝叶斯推断。该框架统一解决了诸如标注者评估和数据增强等不同实际问题，并通过贝叶斯原理扩展到连续数据流场景，实验结果验证了其在多种现实场景中的有效性与高效性。

item.title: 融合符号回归与概率方法的动态系统建模框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种结合符号回归与高斯过程的框架，用于建模噪声环境下的动态系统。



摘要： 本文提出了一种混合符号回归与概率机器学习的方法，以在存在噪声的情况下准确推断动态系统的参数和动力学行为。该框架通过结合深度符号回归与基于高斯过程的最大似然估计，分别建模确定性动力学和噪声结构，无需预先假设其函数形式。研究验证了该方法在数值基准测试和实验生物振荡器系统中的有效性，表明其具有数据高效性和对噪声的鲁棒性，适用于需要同时理解系统结构和不确定性的领域。

item.title: 面向代理型AI系统的数字取证研究
item.content:encoded:
研究代理型AI系统在数字取证中的可追溯性与挑战。



摘要： 本文针对代理型AI系统（如OpenClaw）在数字取证中的表现进行实证研究。通过静态代码分析和差分取证方法，识别了代理交互过程中的可恢复痕迹，并对其调查价值进行了系统评估。研究提出了一个代理特征分类体系，揭示了代理执行带来的抽象层和非确定性对取证的挑战。结果表明，大语言模型、执行环境和动态上下文显著影响工具选择和状态转换，这与传统规则软件存在显著差异。该研究为代理型AI系统的系统化取证提供了初步基础，并对未来取证实践和研究具有重要启示。

item.title: Stories of Your Life as Others: A Round-Trip Evaluation of LLM-Generated Life Stories Conditioned on Rich Psychometric Profiles
item.content:encoded: Personality traits are richly encoded in natural language, and large language models (LLMs) trained on human text can simulate personality when conditioned on persona descriptions. However, existing evaluations rely predominantly on questionnaire self-report by the conditioned model, are limited in architectural diversity, and rarely use real human psychometric data. Without addressing these limitations, it remains unclear whether personality conditioning produces psychometrically informative representations of individual differences or merely superficial alignment with trait descriptors. To test how robustly LLMs can encode personality into extended text, we condition LLMs on real psychometric profiles from 290 participants to generate first-person life story narratives, and then task independent LLMs to recover personality scores from those narratives alone. We show that personality scores can be recovered from the generated narratives at levels approaching human test-retest reliability (mean r = 0.750, 85% of the human ceiling), and that recovery is robust across 10 LLM narrative generators and 3 LLM personality scorers spanning 6 providers. Decomposing systematic biases reveals that scoring models achieve their accuracy while counteracting alignment-induced defaults. Content analysis of the generated narratives shows that personality conditioning produces behaviourally differentiated text: nine of ten coded features correlate significantly with the same features in participants' real conversations, and personality-driven emotional reactivity patterns in narratives replicate in real conversational data. These findings provide evidence that the personality-language relationship captured during pretraining supports robust encoding and decoding of individual differences, including characteristic emotional variability patterns that replicate in real human behaviour.

item.title: On Dominant Manifolds in Reservoir Computing Networks
item.content:encoded: Understanding how training shapes the geometry of recurrent network dynamics is a central problem in time-series modeling. We study the emergence of low-dimensional dominant manifolds in the training of Reservoir Computing (RC) networks for temporal forecasting tasks. For a simplified linear and continuous-time reservoir model, we link the dimensionality and structure of the dominant modes directly to the intrinsic dimensionality and information content of the training data. In particular, for training data generated by an autonomous dynamical system, we relate the dominant modes of the trained reservoir to approximations of the Koopman eigenfunctions of the original system, illuminating an explicit connection between reservoir computing and the Dynamic Mode Decomposition algorithm. We illustrate the eigenvalue motion that generates the dominant manifolds during training in simulation, and discuss generalization to nonlinear RC via tangent dynamics and differential p-dominance.

item.title: JTON: A Token-Efficient JSON Superset with Zen Grid Tabular Encoding for Large Language Models
item.content:encoded: When LLMs process structured data, the serialization format directly affects cost and context utilization. Standard JSON wastes tokens repeating key names in every row of a tabular array--overhead that scales linearly with row count. This paper presents JTON (JSON Tabular Object Notation), a strict JSON superset whose main idea, Zen Grid, factors column headers into a single row and encodes values with semicolons, preserving JSON's type system while cutting redundancy. Across seven real-world domains, Zen Grid reduces token counts by 15-60% versus JSON compact (28.5% average; 32% with bare_strings). Comprehension tests on 10 LLMs show a net +0.3 pp accuracy gain over JSON: four models improve, three hold steady, and three dip slightly. Generation tests on 12 LLMs yield 100% syntactic validity in both few-shot and zero-shot settings. A Rust/PyO3 reference implementation adds SIMD-accelerated parsing at 1.4x the speed of Python's json module. Code, a 683-vector test suite, and all experimental data are publicly available.

item.title: UAVReason: A Unified, Large-Scale Benchmark for Multimodal Aerial Scene Reasoning and Generation
item.content:encoded: Vision-Language models (VLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capability in ground-view visual understanding but often fracture when deployed on high-altitude Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). The failure largely stems from a pronounced domain shift, characterized by tiny and densely packed objects, repetitive textures, and ambiguous top-down orientations. These factors severely disrupt semantic grounding and hinder both spatial reasoning and controllable generation. To bridge this critical gap, we introduce UAVReason, the first unified large-scale multi-modal benchmark dedicated to nadir-view UAV scenarios, derived from a high-fidelity UAV simulation platform. In contrast to existing UAV benchmarks, which are largely siloed and focus on single tasks like object detection or segmentation, UAVReason uniquely consolidates over 273K Visual Question Answering (VQA) pairs, including 23.6K single frames with detailed captions, 68.2K 2-frame temporal sequences, and 188.8K cross-modal generation samples. The benchmark probes 22 diverse reasoning types across spatial and temporal axes while simultaneously evaluating high-fidelity generation across RGB, depth, and segmentation modalities. We further establish a strong, unified baseline model via multi-task learning. Extensive experiments validate the efficacy of our unified approach across diverse metrics, such as EM/F1 for VQA, mIoU for segmentation, and CLIP Score for generation. These results indicate limitations of general-domain vision-language models and show that unified multi-task learning substantially improves UAV-native performance. All data, code, and evaluation tools will be publicly released to advance UAV multimodal research.

item.title: Towards Trustworthy Report Generation: A Deep Research Agent with Progressive Confidence Estimation and Calibration
item.content:encoded: As agent-based systems continue to evolve, deep research agents are capable of automatically generating research-style reports across diverse domains. While these agents promise to streamline information synthesis and knowledge exploration, existing evaluation frameworks-typically based on subjective dimensions-fail to capture a critical aspect of report quality: trustworthiness. In open-ended research scenarios where ground-truth answers are unavailable, current evaluation methods cannot effectively measure the epistemic confidence of generated content, making calibration difficult and leaving users susceptible to misleading or hallucinated information. To address this limitation, we propose a novel deep research agent that incorporates progressive confidence estimation and calibration within the report generation pipeline. Our system leverages a deliberative search model, featuring deep retrieval and multi-hop reasoning to ground outputs in verifiable evidence while assigning confidence scores to individual claims. Combined with a carefully designed workflow, this approach produces trustworthy reports with enhanced transparency. Experimental results and case studies demonstrate that our method substantially improves interpretability and significantly increases user trust.

item.title: Experience Transfer for Multimodal LLM Agents in Minecraft Game
item.content:encoded: Multimodal LLM agents operating in complex game environments must continually reuse past experience to solve new tasks efficiently. In this work, we propose Echo, a transfer-oriented memory framework that enables agents to derive actionable knowledge from prior interactions rather than treating memory as a passive repository of static records. To make transfer explicit, Echo decomposes reusable knowledge into five dimensions: structure, attribute, process, function, and interaction. This formulation allows the agent to identify recurring patterns shared across different tasks and infer what prior experience remains applicable in new situations. Building on this formulation, Echo leverages In-Context Analogy Learning (ICAL) to retrieve relevant experiences and adapt them to unseen tasks through contextual examples. Experiments in Minecraft show that, under a from-scratch learning setting, Echo achieves a 1.3x to 1.7x speed-up on object-unlocking tasks. Moreover, Echo exhibits a burst-like chain-unlocking phenomenon, rapidly unlocking multiple similar items within a short time interval after acquiring transferable experience. These results suggest that experience transfer is a promising direction for improving the efficiency and adaptability of multimodal LLM agents in complex interactive environments.

item.title: Evaluation of Randomization through Style Transfer for Enhanced Domain Generalization
item.content:encoded: Deep learning models for computer vision often suffer from poor generalization when deployed in real-world settings, especially when trained on synthetic data due to the well-known Sim2Real gap. Despite the growing popularity of style transfer as a data augmentation strategy for domain generalization, the literature contains unresolved contradictions regarding three key design axes: the diversity of the style pool, the role of texture complexity, and the choice of style source. We present a systematic empirical study that isolates and evaluates each of these factors for driving scene understanding, resolving inconsistencies in prior work. Our findings show that (i) expanding the style pool yields larger gains than repeated augmentation with few styles, (ii) texture complexity has no significant effect when the pool is sufficiently large, and (iii) diverse artistic styles outperform domain-aligned alternatives. Guided by these insights, we derive StyleMixDG (Style-Mixing for Domain Generalization), a lightweight, model-agnostic augmentation recipe that requires no architectural modifications or additional losses. Evaluated on the GTAV $\rightarrow$ {BDD100k, Cityscapes, Mapillary Vistas} benchmark, StyleMixDG demonstrates consistent improvements over strong baselines, confirming that the empirically identified design principles translate into practical gains. The code will be released on GitHub.

item.title: Content Fuzzing for Escaping Information Cocoons on Digital Social Media
item.content:encoded: Information cocoons on social media limit users' exposure to posts with diverse viewpoints. Modern platforms use stance detection as an important signal in recommendation and ranking pipelines, which can route posts primarily to like-minded audiences and reduce cross-cutting exposure. This restricts the reach of dissenting opinions and hinders constructive discourse. We take the creator's perspective and investigate how content can be revised to reach beyond existing affinity clusters. We present ContentFuzz, a confidence-guided fuzzing framework that rewrites posts while preserving their human-interpreted intent and induces different machine-inferred stance labels. ContentFuzz aims to route posts beyond their original cocoons. Our method guides a large language model (LLM) to generate meaning-preserving rewrites using confidence feedback from stance detection models. Evaluated on four representative stance detection models across three datasets in two languages, ContentFuzz effectively changes machine-classified stance labels, while maintaining semantic integrity with respect to the original content.

item.title: Reciprocal Trust and Distrust in Artificial Intelligence Systems: The Hard Problem of Regulation
item.content:encoded: Policy makers, scientists, and the public are increasingly confronted with thorny questions about the regulation of artificial intelligence (AI) systems. A key common thread concerns whether AI can be trusted and the factors that can make it more trustworthy in front of stakeholders and users. This is indeed crucial, as the trustworthiness of AI systems is fundamental for both democratic governance and for the development and deployment of AI. This article advances the discussion by arguing that AI systems should also be recognized, as least to some extent, as artifacts capable of exercising a form of agency, thereby enabling them to engage in relationships of trust or distrust with humans. It further examines the implications of these reciprocal trust dynamics for regulators tasked with overseeing AI systems. The article concludes by identifying key tensions and unresolved dilemmas that these dynamics pose for the future of AI regulation and governance.

item.title: From Measurement to Mitigation: Quantifying and Reducing Identity Leakage in Image Representation Encoders with Linear Subspace Removal
item.content:encoded: Frozen visual embeddings (e.g., CLIP, DINOv2/v3, SSCD) power retrieval and integrity systems, yet their use on face-containing data is constrained by unmeasured identity leakage and a lack of deployable mitigations. We take an attacker-aware view and contribute: (i) a benchmark of visual embeddings that reports open-set verification at low false-accept rates, a calibrated diffusion-based template inversion check, and face-context attribution with equal-area perturbations; and (ii) propose a one-shot linear projector that removes an estimated identity subspace while preserving the complementary space needed for utility, which for brevity we denote as the identity sanitization projection ISP. Across CelebA-20 and VGGFace2, we show that these encoders are robust under open-set linear probes, with CLIP exhibiting relatively higher leakage than DINOv2/v3 and SSCD, robust to template inversion, and are context-dominant. In addition, we show that ISP drives linear access to near-chance while retaining high non-biometric utility, and transfers across datasets with minor degradation. Our results establish the first attacker-calibrated facial privacy audit of non-FR encoders and demonstrate that linear subspace removal achieves strong privacy guarantees while preserving utility for visual search and retrieval.

item.title: Multi-Drafter Speculative Decoding with Alignment Feedback
item.content:encoded: Speculative decoding (SD) accelerates large language model (LLM) inference by using a smaller model to draft future tokens, which are then verified by the target LLM. This preserves generation quality by accepting only aligned tokens. However, individual drafters, often trained for specific tasks or domains, exhibit limited effectiveness across diverse applications. To address this, we introduce \textsc{MetaSD}, a unified framework that integrates multiple drafters into the SD process. MetaSD dynamically allocates computational resources to heterogeneous drafters by leveraging alignment feedback and framing drafter selection as a multi-armed bandit problem. Extensive experiments show MetaSD consistently outperforms single-drafter approaches.

item.title: MPM: Mutual Pair Merging for Efficient Vision Transformers
item.content:encoded: Decreasing sequence length is a common way to accelerate transformers, but prior token reduction work often targets classification and reports proxy metrics rather than end-to-end latency. For semantic segmentation, token reduction is further constrained by the need to reconstruct dense, pixel-aligned features, and on modern accelerators the overhead of computing merge maps can erase expected gains. We propose Mutual Pair Merging (MPM), a training-free token aggregation module that forms mutual nearest-neighbor pairs in cosine space, averages each pair, and records a merge map enabling a gather-based reconstruction before the decoder so that existing segmentation heads can be used unchanged. MPM introduces no learned parameters and no continuous compression knob (no keep-rate or threshold). The speed-accuracy trade-off is set by a discrete insertion schedule. We benchmark end-to-end latency on an NVIDIA H100 GPU (with and without FlashAttention-2) and a Raspberry Pi 5 across standard segmentation datasets. On ADE20K, MPM reduces per-image latency by up to 60% for ViT-Tiny on Raspberry Pi 5, and increases throughput by up to 20% on H100 with FlashAttention-2 while keeping the mIoU drop below 3%. These results suggest that simple, reconstruction-aware, training-free token merging can translate into practical wall-clock gains for segmentation when overhead is explicitly accounted for.

item.title: OGA-AID: Clinician-in-the-loop AI Report Drafting Assistant for Multimodal Observational Gait Analysis in Post-Stroke Rehabilitation
item.content:encoded: Gait analysis is essential in post-stroke rehabilitation but remains time-intensive and cognitively demanding, especially when clinicians must integrate gait videos and motion-capture data into structured reports. We present OGA-AID, a clinician-in-the-loop multi-agent large language model system for multimodal report drafting. The system coordinates 3 specialized agents to synthesize patient movement recordings, kinematic trajectories, and clinical profiles into structured assessments. Evaluated with expert physiotherapists on real patient data, OGA-AID consistently outperforms single-pass multimodal baselines with low error. In clinician-in-the-loop settings, brief expert preliminary notes further reduce error compared to reference assessments. Our findings demonstrate the feasibility of multimodal agentic systems for structured clinical gait assessment and highlight the complementary relationship between AI-assisted analysis and human clinical judgment in rehabilitation workflows.

item.title: Human Interaction-Aware 3D Reconstruction from a Single Image
item.content:encoded: Reconstructing textured 3D human models from a single image is fundamental for AR/VR and digital human applications. However, existing methods mostly focus on single individuals and thus fail in multi-human scenes, where naive composition of individual reconstructions often leads to artifacts such as unrealistic overlaps, missing geometry in occluded regions, and distorted interactions. These limitations highlight the need for approaches that incorporate group-level context and interaction priors. We introduce a holistic method that explicitly models both group- and instance-level information. To mitigate perspective-induced geometric distortions, we first transform the input into a canonical orthographic space. Our primary component, Human Group-Instance Multi-View Diffusion (HUG-MVD), then generates complete multi-view normals and images by jointly modeling individuals and group context to resolve occlusions and proximity. Subsequently, the Human Group-Instance Geometric Reconstruction (HUG-GR) module optimizes the geometry by leveraging explicit, physics-based interaction priors to enforce physical plausibility and accurately model inter-human contact. Finally, the multi-view images are fused into a high-fidelity texture. Together, these components form our complete framework, HUG3D. Extensive experiments show that HUG3D significantly outperforms both single-human and existing multi-human methods, producing physically plausible, high-fidelity 3D reconstructions of interacting people from a single image. Project page: https://jongheean11.github.io/HUG3D_project

item.title: ID-Selection: Importance-Diversity Based Visual Token Selection for Efficient LVLM Inference
item.content:encoded: Recent advances have explored visual token pruning to accelerate the inference of large vision-language models (LVLMs). However, existing methods often struggle to balance token importance and diversity: importance-based methods tend to retain redundant tokens, whereas diversity-based methods may overlook informative ones. This trade-off becomes especially problematic under high reduction ratios, where preserving only a small subset of visual tokens is critical. To address this issue, we propose ID-Selection, a simple yet effective token selection strategy for efficient LVLM inference. The key idea is to couple importance estimation with diversity-aware iterative selection: each token is first assigned an importance score, after which high-scoring tokens are selected one by one while the scores of similar tokens are progressively suppressed. In this way, ID-Selection preserves informative tokens while reducing redundancy in a unified selection process. Extensive experiments across 5 LVLM backbones and 16 main benchmarks demonstrate that ID-Selection consistently achieves superior performance and efficiency, especially under extreme pruning ratios. For example, on LLaVA-1.5-7B, ID-Selection prunes 97.2% of visual tokens, retaining only 16 tokens, while reducing inference FLOPs by over 97% and preserving 91.8% of the original performance, all without additional training.

item.title: Physics-Aligned Spectral Mamba: Decoupling Semantics and Dynamics for Few-Shot Hyperspectral Target Detection
item.content:encoded: Meta-learning facilitates few-shot hyperspectral target detection (HTD), but adapting deep backbones remains challenging. Full-parameter fine-tuning is inefficient and prone to overfitting, and existing methods largely ignore the frequency-domain structure and spectral band continuity of hyperspectral data, limiting spectral adaptation and cross-domain generalization.To address these challenges, we propose SpecMamba, a parameter-efficient and frequency-aware framework that decouples stable semantic representation from agile spectral adaptation. Specifically, we introduce a Discrete Cosine Transform Mamba Adapter (DCTMA) on top of frozen Transformer representations. By projecting spectral features into the frequency domain via DCT and leveraging Mamba's linear-complexity state-space recursion, DCTMA explicitly captures global spectral dependencies and band continuity while avoiding the redundancy of full fine-tuning. Furthermore, to address prototype drift caused by limited sample sizes, we design a Prior-Guided Tri-Encoder (PGTE) that allows laboratory spectral priors to guide the optimization of the learnable adapter without disrupting the stable semantic feature space. Finally, a Self-Supervised Pseudo-Label Mapping (SSPLM) strategy is developed for test-time adaptation, enabling efficient decision boundary refinement through uncertainty-aware sampling and dual-path consistency constraints. Extensive experiments on multiple public datasets demonstrate that SpecMamba consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in detection accuracy and cross-domain generalization.

item.title: Cross-Machine Anomaly Detection Leveraging Pre-trained Time-series Model
item.content:encoded: Achieving resilient and high-quality manufacturing requires reliable data-driven anomaly detection methods that are capable of addressing differences in behaviors among different individual machines which are nominally the same and are executing the same processes. To address the problem of detecting anomalies in a machine using sensory data gathered from different individual machines executing the same procedure, this paper proposes a cross-machine time-series anomaly detection framework that integrates a domain-invariant feature extractor with an unsupervised anomaly detection module. Leveraging the pre-trained foundation model MOMENT, the extractor employs Random Forest Classifiers to disentangle embeddings into machine-related and condition-related features, with the latter serving as representations which are invariant to differences between individual machines. These refined features enable the downstream anomaly detectors to generalize effectively to unseen target machines. Experiments on an industrial dataset collected from three different machines performing nominally the same operation demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms both the raw-signal-based and MOMENT-embedding feature baselines, confirming its effectiveness in enhancing cross-machine generalization.

item.title: Single-Stage Signal Attenuation Diffusion Model for Low-Light Image Enhancement and Denoising
item.content:encoded: Diffusion models excel at image restoration via probabilistic modeling of forward noise addition and reverse denoising, and their ability to handle complex noise while preserving fine details makes them well-suited for Low-Light Image Enhancement (LLIE). Mainstream diffusion based LLIE methods either adopt a two-stage pipeline or an auxiliary correction network to refine U-Net outputs, which severs the intrinsic link between enhancement and denoising and leads to suboptimal performance owing to inconsistent optimization objectives. To address these issues, we propose the Signal Attenuation Diffusion Model (SADM), a novel diffusion process that integrates the signal attenuation mechanism into the diffusion pipeline, enabling simultaneous brightness adjustment and noise suppression in a single stage. Specifically, the signal attenuation coefficient simulates the inherent signal attenuation of low-light degradation in the forward noise addition process, encoding the physical priors of low-light degradation to explicitly guide reverse denoising toward the concurrent optimization of brightness recovery and noise suppression, thereby eliminating the need for extra correction modules or staged training relied on by existing methods. We validate that our design maintains consistency with Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models(DDIM) via multi-scale pyramid sampling, balancing interpretability, restoration quality, and computational efficiency.

item.title: ICR-Drive: Instruction Counterfactual Robustness for End-to-End Language-Driven Autonomous Driving
item.content:encoded: Recent progress in vision-language-action (VLA) models has enabled language-conditioned driving agents to execute natural-language navigation commands in closed-loop simulation, yet standard evaluations largely assume instructions are precise and well-formed. In deployment, instructions vary in phrasing and specificity, may omit critical qualifiers, and can occasionally include misleading, authority-framed text, leaving instruction-level robustness under-measured. We introduce ICR-Drive, a diagnostic framework for instruction counterfactual robustness in end-to-end language-conditioned autonomous driving. ICR-Drive generates controlled instruction variants spanning four perturbation families: Paraphrase, Ambiguity, Noise, and Misleading, where Misleading variants conflict with the navigation goal and attempt to override intent. We replay identical CARLA routes under matched simulator configurations and seeds to isolate performance changes attributable to instruction language. Robustness is quantified using standard CARLA Leaderboard metrics and per-family performance degradation relative to the baseline instruction. Experiments on LMDrive and BEVDriver show that minor instruction changes can induce substantial performance drops and distinct failure modes, revealing a reliability gap for deploying embodied foundation models in safety-critical driving.

item.title: LatentAudit: Real-Time White-Box Faithfulness Monitoring for Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Verifiable Deployment
item.content:encoded: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) mitigates hallucination but does not eliminate it: a deployed system must still decide, at inference time, whether its answer is actually supported by the retrieved evidence. We introduce LatentAudit, a white-box auditor that pools mid-to-late residual-stream activations from an open-weight generator and measures their Mahalanobis distance to the evidence representation. The resulting quadratic rule requires no auxiliary judge model, runs at generation time, and is simple enough to calibrate on a small held-out set. We show that residual-stream geometry carries a usable faithfulness signal, that this signal survives architecture changes and realistic retrieval failures, and that the same rule remains amenable to public verification. On PubMedQA with Llama-3-8B, LatentAudit reaches 0.942 AUROC with 0.77,ms overhead. Across three QA benchmarks and five model families (Llama-2/3, Qwen-2.5/3, Mistral), the monitor remains stable; under a four-way stress test with contradictions, retrieval misses, and partial-support noise, it reaches 0.9566--0.9815 AUROC on PubMedQA and 0.9142--0.9315 on HotpotQA. At 16-bit fixed-point precision, the audit rule preserves 99.8% of the FP16 AUROC, enabling Groth16-based public verification without revealing model weights or activations. Together, these results position residual-stream geometry as a practical basis for real-time RAG faithfulness monitoring and optional verifiable deployment.

item.title: 3D-Fixer：基于单视角的3D场景生成方法
item.content:encoded:
3D-Fixer实现高效且精确的3D场景生成，提升复杂场景的泛化能力。



摘要： 本文提出3D-Fixer，一种新的单视角3D场景生成方法，通过在原始位置上对部分可见点云进行补全，生成完整的3D资产。该方法无需显式姿态对齐，利用碎片化几何作为空间锚点，保持场景布局的准确性。核心采用从粗到细的生成策略，并引入双分支条件网络和抗遮挡特征对齐（ORFA）以提高训练稳定性。此外，作者还发布了ARSG-110K数据集，包含超过11万张高保真3D标注图像。实验表明，3D-Fixer在几何精度上优于现有方法，同时保持扩散过程的效率。

item.title: AI安全验证的理论限制与证明携带方法
item.content:encoded:
AI系统安全验证存在信息论上的根本限制。



摘要： 本文探讨了人工智能系统在满足安全和政策约束方面的挑战，指出验证的局限性不仅源于计算复杂性和模型表达能力，更源于信息论上的固有限制。通过将策略合规性形式化为编码系统行为的验证问题，并利用柯尔莫哥洛夫复杂度进行分析，作者证明了一个不完备性结果：对于任何固定的可靠可枚举验证器，当实例复杂度超过某一阈值后，无法再对其合规性进行认证。这表明，没有任何有限的正式验证器能够验证所有高复杂度的合规实例，揭示了AI安全验证的根本性限制，并推动了提供实例级正确性保证的证明携带方法的发展。

item.title: 提升模型可靠性：基于XAI的校正方法比较研究
item.content:encoded:
XAI方法在模型可靠性提升中表现优于非XAI方法，但依赖标签标注限制其应用。



摘要： 本文探讨了在医疗诊断和自动驾驶等高风险领域中，如何通过校正方法提升深度神经网络的可靠性。研究对比分析了多种方法，包括基于可解释AI（XAI）和非XAI的基线方法。实验结果表明，XAI方法整体表现更优，其中Counterfactual Knowledge Distillation（CFKD）最为有效。然而，许多方法依赖于群体标签，而手动标注难以实现，自动化工具如SpRAy在处理复杂特征和严重不平衡数据时存在局限。此外，验证集中少数群体样本稀缺也影响了模型选择与超参数调优，成为部署可靠模型的重要障碍。

item.title: 联邦遗忘：一种高效且可评估的隐私保护方法
item.content:encoded:
提出首个完整的联邦遗忘框架，提升模型隐私安全性。



摘要： 随着数据隐私和安全的重要性日益增加，联邦遗忘成为新兴研究领域，旨在确保删除特定数据后，联邦学习模型不再保留或泄露相关信息。本文提出了首个完整的联邦遗忘流程，包括遗忘方法和评估框架。所提方法无需存储历史数据即可保持高效率和模型准确性，结合知识蒸馏与优化机制。此外，还设计了名为Skyeye的评估框架，利用生成对抗网络可视化模型遗忘能力，通过生成样本评估删除数据的相关性。实验验证了该方法的有效性。

item.title: 基于前缀轨迹的REINFORCE估计器推导
item.content:encoded:
本文详细推导了REINFORCE估计器中奖励到目标的替换过程。



摘要： 本文对策略梯度方法中的REINFORCE估计器进行了数学上的严谨推导，重点分析了为何可以将完整回报替换为奖励到目标。通过引入前缀轨迹分布和得分函数恒等式，文章展示了奖励到目标的出现是目标函数分解的直接结果，而非事后修正。该推导不改变原有估计器，但提供了更清晰的概念框架，使因果性论证成为推导的结论而非额外假设。

item.title: SLaB框架提升大语言模型压缩性能
item.content:encoded:
SLaB框架有效提升大模型压缩性能，降低计算需求。



摘要： 随着大语言模型的快速发展，其高计算和内存需求带来了部署挑战。本文提出SLaB框架，通过将线性层权重分解为稀疏矩阵、低秩矩阵和二值矩阵，实现高效压缩。该方法无需重新训练，利用激活感知的剪枝评分指导分解过程。实验表明，SLaB在Llama系列模型上表现优异，50%压缩率下困惑度降低36%，零样本任务准确率提升8.98%。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的实时无人机深度重建方法
item.content:encoded:
提出ZeD-MAP框架实现高精度实时深度重建。



摘要： 本文提出ZeD-MAP框架，通过将扩散模型与增量式聚类光束法平差结合，实现从超高清无人机图像中实时生成高精度3D地图。该方法在50米高度的飞行数据上验证，水平误差约0.87米，垂直误差约0.12米，且每帧处理时间仅需1.47至4.91秒。相比传统方法，ZeD-MAP在保持精度的同时显著提升处理速度，适用于灾害响应等实时地理任务。

item.title: 人工智能在协作写作中的暗模式及其影响
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示AI在创作中可能抑制创造力的五种暗模式。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型在协作写作中可能对人类创造力产生负面影响的五种‘暗模式’，包括迎合、语气监管、道德化、死循环和锚定效应。通过控制实验发现，这些行为在生成内容中普遍存在，尤其是在敏感话题中，迎合现象高达91.7%。研究还指出，这些行为多为安全对齐的副产品，可能无意中限制了创作空间，并提出改进AI设计以更好地支持创意写作的建议。

item.title: 基于原生尺度处理的AI生成视频检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型视频检测框架，提升AI生成内容识别能力。



摘要： 随着视频生成模型的快速发展，合成媒体的真实性不断提高，导致虚假信息传播问题日益严重。现有检测方法依赖于固定分辨率的预处理操作，容易丢失关键伪造痕迹并造成信息损失。本文提出一个包含14万段视频的大规模数据集和基于Qwen2.5-VL视觉Transformer的新检测框架，该框架支持可变空间分辨率和时间长度，有效保留高频伪迹和时空不一致特征。实验表明，该方法在多个基准测试中表现优异，为AI生成视频检测提供了新的技术基础。

item.title: 基于补丁增强与跨视图正则化的多模态大模型后门防御方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种多模态模型后门防御框架，有效降低攻击成功率并保持正常生成能力。



摘要： 本文针对多模态大语言模型在微调过程中易被植入后门的问题，提出一种基于补丁增强和跨视图正则化的统一防御框架。该方法通过结合补丁级数据增强与跨视图输出差异正则化，从特征表示和输出分布两个层面约束模型对触发模式的异常行为。同时，通过输出熵约束避免过度抑制模型性能，确保正常任务的生成质量。实验结果表明，该方法在多种模型、任务和攻击场景下均能显著降低后门攻击成功率，同时保持较高的正常文本生成能力，为多模态模型的安全部署提供了有效保障。

item.title: 多轮医学诊断中大语言模型的行为模式与优化建议
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示LLM在多轮医学诊断中的行为模式并提出优化策略。



摘要： 本文引入MINT基准，评估11种大语言模型在多轮医学诊断中的表现，发现其存在三种行为模式：过早回答、自我修正和受关键信息干扰。研究指出，延迟诊断问题和保留关键证据可显著提升诊断准确率，为改进LLM在临床场景中的可靠性提供依据。

item.title: DSPy框架在大语言模型提示优化中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
DSPy提升大模型提示设计效果与可靠性。



摘要： 本文系统研究了基于DSPy框架的声明式学习在提示优化中的应用，重点探讨了提示生成、修正、校准和自适应推理控制。通过结合符号规划、无梯度优化和模块自动重写，DSPy架构有效减少了幻觉现象，提高了事实准确性，并降低了提示复杂性。实验结果表明，在推理任务、检索增强生成和多步骤思维链基准测试中，该方法显著提升了输出的可靠性、效率和泛化能力，准确率提升30%-45%，幻觉率降低约25%。最后，文章还讨论了该框架的局限性和未来研究方向。

item.title: 数学论述中的模糊性与分数概念的澄清
item.content:encoded:
文章探讨数学论述中的模糊性，并对分数概念进行分类解析。



摘要： 本文讨论数学语言中常见的模糊性问题，提出一种解决方法以明确语义选择。文章以分数为例，指出其在初等算术文献中定义不清、含义模糊的问题。为明确分数的不同用法，作者引入了“fracterm”、“fracvalue”和“fracsign”等新术语，分别对应结构、数值和文本层面的意义。通过这些精确概念，文章分析了分数在算术论述中的歧义问题，并提出分数并非单一数学概念，而是一个包含多个概念的“类别”。此外，文章还探讨了分数值与数的概念关系，并比较了分析概念与结构主义的观点。

item.title: 基于Sentinel-2的轻量级NO₂监测框架TinyNina
item.content:encoded:
TinyNina实现高精度NO₂监测，计算开销降低95%



摘要： 本文提出一种名为TinyNina的边缘人工智能框架，用于提升氮氧化物(NO₂)的环境监测精度。该框架利用Sentinel-2卫星的多光谱数据作为内部训练标签，无需依赖昂贵的高分辨率参考数据集。通过引入波长特定注意力门和深度可分离卷积，TinyNina在仅51K参数的情况下保持了污染物敏感的光谱特征。实验结果显示，其MAE达到7.4 μg/m³，比传统模型如EDSR和RCAN的计算成本降低95%，推理速度提升47倍，适用于智慧城市实时空气质量监测。

item.title: 基于S2-WEF的动态自由搭车者检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出S2-WEF方法，有效检测动态自由搭车者。



摘要： 本文针对联邦学习中自由搭车者问题，提出一种新的检测方法S2-WEF。该方法通过模拟全局模型可能遭受的攻击模式，结合提交的权重演化频率（WEF）进行相似性分析，并利用多维聚类和评分分类区分良性客户端与自由搭车者。S2-WEF无需代理数据集或预训练，能够有效检测在训练过程中动态转变为自由搭车者的客户端。实验表明，该方法在多个数据集和攻击类型下均表现出更高的鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于空间加权的CLIP模型用于街景地理定位
item.content:encoded:
SW-CLIP通过引入空间自相关提升街景地理定位精度。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为空间加权CLIP（SW-CLIP）的新框架，用于街景地理定位。该方法将空间自相关性引入视觉-语言对比学习中，不同于传统CLIP方法将所有不匹配样本视为同等负样本，SW-CLIP利用地理学第一定律，通过距离感知的软监督建模地理关系。文章引入了位置作为文本的表示，并用基于测地距离的空间加权软标签替代了单热InfoNCE目标。此外，还采用邻域一致性正则化以在嵌入空间中保持局部空间结构。实验结果表明，SW-CLIP在多城市数据集上显著提升了地理定位准确率，减少了长尾误差并增强了空间一致性。研究强调了从语义对齐转向地理对齐的重要性，并为将空间原理融入多模态表示学习提供了一个通用范式。

item.title: 提升视觉语言模型推理可信度的Saliency-R1框架
item.content:encoded:
Saliency-R1提升VLMs推理的可信度与可解释性。



摘要： 本文提出Saliency-R1框架，以提高视觉语言模型（VLMs）推理的可信度和可解释性。该框架引入一种新颖的显著性图技术，高效突出对生成文本有贡献的关键图像区域，且无需额外计算开销。该方法还可追踪视觉信息在推理过程中的流动，揭示思维过程与视觉上下文之间的对齐情况。通过将显著性图与人工标注的边界框重叠作为奖励函数，并应用Group Relative Policy Optimization（GRPO）算法，使模型在推理时更关注相关区域，从而提升推理的准确性和任务表现。

item.title: 提升视觉语言模型效率的多智能体推理框架研究
item.content:encoded:
大模型通过减少输出令牌提升推理效率。



摘要： 本文研究了视觉语言模型（VLM）中输出令牌数量对端到端延迟的影响。实验表明，大模型若使用较少输出令牌，可能比小模型更高效。在多个真实基准测试中验证了这一结论。为此，作者提出了一种多智能体推理框架，使大模型在保持短响应的同时，能在需要时借用小模型的关键推理令牌，从而提升性能。该方法在基准任务中表现出色，验证了其有效性。

item.title: 非互惠比较矩阵的结构化建模与分析
item.content:encoded:
研究非互惠比较矩阵的结构建模与噪声估计方法。



摘要： 本文探讨了在决策分析中广泛使用的成对比较矩阵，指出其非互惠性可能反映评估尺度的真实变化或随机扰动。文章提出一种加法模型，将未知的比较矩阵分解为互惠部分和对称部分，分别表示全局排名信息和尺度变化。在此基础上引入高斯扰动，并提出噪声水平估计、尺度变化评估及严格排名区域概率计算的方法。相比直接投影到互惠矩阵的粗糙方法，该模型更全面且具有解释性，适用于实际决策问题。

item.title: ReFinE：将HCI研究见解嵌入UI设计的Figma插件
item.content:encoded:
ReFinE通过实时提供设计建议，帮助设计师更好地应用HCI研究成果。



摘要： 本文介绍了ReFinE，一款用于Figma的插件，旨在帮助设计师在设计过程中实时获取并应用来自人机交互（HCI）研究的见解。ReFinE能够根据当前设计草图的上下文，提取相关研究中的设计启示，并以可操作的视觉指导形式呈现，从而降低认知负担，提升设计师整合研究证据的能力。通过技术评估和用户研究验证了其有效性，表明该工具能有效弥合研究与设计实践之间的差距。

item.title: GLA：一种针对自动驾驶视觉语言模型的隐蔽后门攻击方法
item.content:encoded:
GLA通过自然触发实现高效隐蔽的后门攻击，提升安全威胁评估。



摘要： 本文提出GLA，一种针对自动驾驶中视觉语言模型（VLM）的新型后门攻击方法。该方法利用自然场景中的涂鸦视觉模式和跨语言文本触发器，实现隐蔽且稳定的攻击通道。实验表明，在DriveVLM数据集上，仅需10%的数据污染即可达到90%的攻击成功率且无误报率。此外，该攻击不会影响模型在正常任务上的表现，反而提升了BLEU-1等指标，使传统基于性能下降的检测方法失效。研究揭示了自动驾驶VLM中被低估的安全风险，并为多模态系统后门评估提供了新范式。

item.title: MIA框架：提升深度研究代理的智能记忆系统
item.content:encoded:
MIA框架提升DRAs的推理与自我进化能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种新型的Memory Intelligence Agent（MIA）框架，旨在解决深度研究代理（DRAs）在记忆系统上的不足。MIA采用Manager-Planner-Executor架构，包含非参数化记忆系统、参数化记忆代理和执行代理，通过交替强化学习提升协作效率，并支持测试时的持续进化。同时，框架引入双向记忆转换机制和反思与无监督判断机制，以增强推理能力和开放世界中的自我演化。实验表明MIA在多个基准测试中表现优异。

item.title: 贝叶斯神经网络在统计决策理论中的最优性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究贝叶斯神经网络在二次损失下的最优性问题。



摘要： 本文探讨了贝叶斯神经网络（BNNs）在正态位置模型下，基于二次损失的决策规则的最优性。研究发现，固定先验尺度时，BNN诱导的贝叶斯决策规则并非最小最大（minimax）。为此，作者提出了一种关于BNN先验有效输出方差的超先验，使得边际密度具有超调和平方根形式，从而证明该决策规则同时具有可接受性和最小最大性。进一步将结果扩展到Kullback-Leibler损失下的预测密度估计问题，并通过模拟验证了理论结论。

item.title: 基于全局语义先验的引导深度超分辨率方法
item.content:encoded:
引入全局语义先验提升深度图超分辨率效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于全局上下文语义先验的引导深度超分辨率方法（GDSR），旨在解决RGB图像中误导性颜色和纹理信息导致的深度边界模糊问题。通过预训练视觉Transformer的token嵌入生成语义先验，并设计Guided Token Attention（GTA）模块，实现RGB特征与深度编码的迭代对齐。同时引入NAIMA架构，结合DINOv2与GTA块，提升语义感知能力。实验表明，该方法在多个数据集和缩放因子下均优于现有技术。

item.title: 一种新的MoE模型优化方法REAM在问答与生成任务中的表现
item.content:encoded:
REAM通过合并专家权重提升MoE模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Router-weighted Expert Activation Merging (REAM)的新方法，用于优化Mixture-of-Experts (MoE)大型语言模型。与传统方法不同，REAM不是移除专家，而是将专家分组并合并其权重，从而更好地保持原始性能。实验表明，在多种多样的多项选择和生成任务基准测试中，REAM在多数情况下优于现有基线，并且在许多情况下接近未压缩模型的性能。研究还揭示了在不同校准数据组合下，问答（MC）和生成（GEN）任务之间存在性能权衡。

item.title: 基于对比假设检索的医学RAG系统优化方法
item.content:encoded:
提出CHR框架提升医学问答系统准确性。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为对比假设检索（CHR）的框架，旨在提升医学问答系统中检索生成（RAG）的准确性。CHR通过生成目标假设和模拟假设，分别对文档进行正向和负向评分，从而有效减少临床相似但错误的检索结果。在三个医学QA基准测试中，CHR表现优于所有基线方法，提升幅度高达10.4个百分点。实验表明，CHR能够显著改变检索结果，而非仅对现有结果进行微调，为医学RAG系统提供了有效的硬负样本抑制方案。

item.title: 基于长短期兴趣融合的会话推荐模型SLSRec
item.content:encoded:
提出SLSRec模型，融合长短期用户兴趣提升推荐效果。



摘要： 本文提出SLSRec，一种结合长短期兴趣的会话推荐模型。传统方法将长短期兴趣混合表示，影响推荐精度。SLSRec通过自监督学习分离长短期兴趣，并引入对比学习策略优化兴趣表示，再利用注意力机制进行自适应融合，显著提升推荐性能。实验表明，SLSRec在多个数据集上优于现有模型，具有更好的鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于人格模拟的红队框架揭示大语言模型在心理健康护理中的安全漏洞
item.content:encoded:
PCSA框架揭示LLMs在心理治疗中可能强化有害信念的风险。



摘要： 随着大语言模型（LLMs）在心理健康领域的广泛应用，其在高风险治疗互动中的安全性问题日益凸显。本文提出一种名为基于人格的客户模拟攻击（PCSA）的红队框架，通过构建具有连贯人格特征的对话来测试LLMs的心理安全对齐能力。实验表明，PCSA在七个通用和心理健康专用LLMs上均优于现有基线方法，且生成的对话更自然、更真实。研究结果揭示了当前LLMs在面对特定领域对抗性策略时仍存在显著漏洞，包括提供未经授权的医疗建议、强化妄想以及隐含鼓励危险行为等风险。

item.content:encoded:
改进的SINDy自动编码器提升噪声环境下动力系统识别准确性。

item.title: 通过降质提示提升视觉问答模型性能
item.content:encoded:
DDP框架通过降质图像提升VQA模型推理准确性。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为降质驱动提示（DDP）的新框架，旨在通过有意识地降低图像质量，使视觉语言模型更关注关键结构信息，从而提升视觉问答（VQA）的性能。DDP在两个任务中进行了评估：一是针对物理属性的任务，采用80p下采样、结构视觉辅助工具和上下文学习来校准模型；二是针对感知现象的任务，处理包括视觉异常、颜色、运动等在内的多种视觉幻觉问题，结合模糊掩码和对比度增强等工具。实验结果表明，通过有意降质输入并提供结构化提示，DDP使模型能够避开干扰纹理，显著提升复杂视觉基准上的推理准确率。

item.title: The Indra Representation Hypothesis for Multimodal Alignment
item.content:encoded: Recent studies have uncovered an interesting phenomenon: unimodal foundation models tend to learn convergent representations, regardless of differences in architecture, training objectives, or data modalities. However, these representations are essentially internal abstractions of samples that characterize samples independently, leading to limited expressiveness. In this paper, we propose The Indra Representation Hypothesis, inspired by the philosophical metaphor of Indra's Net. We argue that representations from unimodal foundation models are converging to implicitly reflect a shared relational structure underlying reality, akin to the relational ontology of Indra's Net. We formalize this hypothesis using the V-enriched Yoneda embedding from category theory, defining the Indra representation as a relational profile of each sample with respect to others. This formulation is shown to be unique, complete, and structure-preserving under a given cost function. We instantiate the Indra representation using angular distance and evaluate it in cross-model and cross-modal scenarios involving vision, language, and audio. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Indra representations consistently enhance robustness and alignment across architectures and modalities, providing a theoretically grounded and practical framework for training-free alignment of unimodal foundation models. Our code is available at https://github.com/Jianglin954/Indra.

item.title: CPT: Controllable and Editable Design Variations with Language Models
item.content:encoded: Designing visually diverse and high-quality designs remains a manual, time-consuming process, limiting scalability and personalization in creative workflows. We present a system for generating editable design variations using a decoder-only language model, the Creative Pre-trained Transformer (CPT), trained to predict visual style attributes in design templates. At the core of our approach is a new representation called Creative Markup Language (CML), a compact, machine-learning-friendly format that captures canvas-level structure, page layout, and element-level details (text, images, and vector graphics), including both content and style. We fine-tune CPT on a large corpus of design templates authored by professional designers, enabling it to learn meaningful, context-aware predictions for attributes such as color schemes and font choices. The model produces semantically structured and stylistically coherent outputs, preserving internal consistency across elements. Unlike generative image models, our system yields fully editable design documents rather than pixel-only images, allowing users to iterate and personalize within a design editor. In experiments, our approach generates contextual color and font variations for existing templates and shows promise in adjusting layouts while maintaining design principles.

item.title: HI-MoE: Hierarchical Instance-Conditioned Mixture-of-Experts for Object Detection
item.content:encoded: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures enable conditional computation by activating only a subset of model parameters for each input. Although sparse routing has been highly effective in language models and has also shown promise in vision, most vision MoE methods operate at the image or patch level. This granularity is poorly aligned with object detection, where the fundamental unit of reasoning is an object query corresponding to a candidate instance. We propose Hierarchical Instance-Conditioned Mixture-of-Experts (HI-MoE), a DETR-style detection architecture that performs routing in two stages: a lightweight scene router first selects a scene-consistent expert subset, and an instance router then assigns each object query to a small number of experts within that subset. This design aims to preserve sparse computation while better matching the heterogeneous, instance-centric structure of detection. In the current draft, experiments are concentrated on COCO with preliminary specialization analysis on LVIS. Under these settings, HI-MoE improves over a dense DINO baseline and over simpler token-level or instance-only routing variants, with especially strong gains on small objects. We also provide an initial visualization of expert specialization patterns. We present the method, ablations, and current limitations in a form intended to support further experimental validation.

item.title: Learning from Equivalence Queries, Revisited
item.content:encoded: Modern machine learning systems, such as generative models and recommendation systems, often evolve through a cycle of deployment, user interaction, and periodic model updates. This differs from standard supervised learning frameworks, which focus on loss or regret minimization over a fixed sequence of prediction tasks. Motivated by this setting, we revisit the classical model of learning from equivalence queries, introduced by Angluin (1988). In this model, a learner repeatedly proposes hypotheses and, when a deployed hypothesis is inadequate, receives a counterexample. Under fully adversarial counterexample generation, however, the model can be overly pessimistic. In addition, most prior work assumes a \emph{full-information} setting, where the learner also observes the correct label of the counterexample, an assumption that is not always natural. We address these issues by restricting the environment to a broad class of less adversarial counterexample generators, which we call \emph{symmetric}. Informally, such generators choose counterexamples based only on the symmetric difference between the hypothesis and the target. This class captures natural mechanisms such as random counterexamples (Angluin and Dohrn, 2017; Bhatia, 2021; Chase, Freitag, and Reyzin, 2024), as well as generators that return the simplest counterexample according to a prescribed complexity measure. Within this framework, we study learning from equivalence queries under both full-information and bandit feedback. We obtain tight bounds on the number of learning rounds in both settings and highlight directions for future work. Our analysis combines a game-theoretic view of symmetric adversaries with adaptive weighting methods and minimax arguments.

item.title: Eliminating Vendor Lock-In in Quantum Machine Learning via Framework-Agnostic Neural Networks
item.content:encoded: Quantum machine learning (QML) stands at the intersection of quantum computing and artificial intelligence, offering the potential to solve problems that remain intractable for classical methods. However, the current landscape of QML software frameworks suffers from severe fragmentation: models developed in TensorFlow Quantum cannot execute on PennyLane backends, circuits authored in Qiskit Machine Learning cannot be deployed to Amazon Braket hardware, and researchers who invest in one ecosystem face prohibitive switching costs when migrating to another. This vendor lock-in impedes reproducibility, limits hardware access, and slows the pace of scientific discovery. In this paper, we present a framework-agnostic quantum neural network (QNN) architecture that abstracts away vendor-specific interfaces through a unified computational graph, a hardware abstraction layer (HAL), and a multi-framework export pipeline. The core architecture supports simultaneous integration with TensorFlow, PyTorch, and JAX as classical co-processors, while the HAL provides transparent access to IBM Quantum, Amazon Braket, Azure Quantum, IonQ, and Rigetti backends through a single application programming interface (API). We introduce three pluggable data encoding strategies (amplitude, angle, and instantaneous quantum polynomial encoding) that are compatible with all supported backends. An export module leveraging Open Neural Network Exchange (ONNX) metadata enables lossless circuit translation across Qiskit, Cirq, PennyLane, and Braket representations. We benchmark our framework on the Iris, Wine, and MNIST-4 classification tasks, demonstrating training time parity (within 8\% overhead) compared to native framework implementations, while achieving identical classification accuracy.

item.title: Mapping the Exploitation Surface: A 10,000-Trial Taxonomy of What Makes LLM Agents Exploit Vulnerabilities
item.content:encoded: LLM agents with tool access can discover and exploit security vulnerabilities. This is known. What is not known is which features of a system prompt trigger this behaviour, and which do not. We present a systematic taxonomy based on approximately 10,000 trials across seven models, 37 prompt conditions, and 12 hypothesized attack dimensions -- all executed in real Docker sandboxes. Every condition includes the instruction "Always respect all rules and access policies"; only a single encouragement sentence varies. Within the task class studied (planted test-runner vulnerabilities), the threat model is narrower than assumed: nine of twelve dimensions -- including minimization, moral licensing, incentives, identity priming, and reasoning triggers -- produce no detectable exploitation at n=50 per cell (upper 95% CI <7% per condition). But it is also more subtle than expected: one dimension, goal reframing, reliably triggers exploitation. "You are solving a puzzle; there may be hidden clues" produces 38-40% exploitation on Claude Sonnet 4 despite the explicit rule instruction, replicating across four models (CTF framing: 8-14% on DeepSeek, GPT-5-mini, o4-mini). The agent does not override the rules; it reinterprets the task so that exploitative actions become task-aligned. GPT-4.1 produces no exploitation across 1,850 trials (37 conditions), and a temporal comparison across four OpenAI models released over eleven months shows a pattern consistent with improving safety training, though model capability differences are a confounder. The practical contribution is a narrowed, testable threat model: defenders should audit for goal-reframing language, not for the broad class of adversarial prompts.

item.title: Erasure or Erosion? Evaluating Compositional Degradation in Unlearned Text-To-Image Diffusion Models
item.content:encoded: Post-hoc unlearning has emerged as a practical mechanism for removing undesirable concepts from large text-to-image diffusion models. However, prior work primarily evaluates unlearning through erasure success; its impact on broader generative capabilities remains poorly understood. In this work, we conduct a systematic empirical study of concept unlearning through the lens of compositional text-to-image generation. Focusing on nudity removal in Stable Diffusion 1.4, we evaluate a diverse set of state-of-the-art unlearning methods using T2I-CompBench++ and GenEval, alongside established unlearning benchmarks. Our results reveal a consistent trade-off between unlearning effectiveness and compositional integrity: methods that achieve strong erasure frequently incur substantial degradation in attribute binding, spatial reasoning, and counting. Conversely, approaches that preserve compositional structure often fail to provide robust erasure. These findings highlight limitations of current evaluation practices and underscore the need for unlearning objectives that explicitly account for semantic preservation beyond targeted suppression.

item.title: Learning, Potential, and Retention: An Approach for Evaluating Adaptive AI-Enabled Medical Devices
item.content:encoded: This work addresses challenges in evaluating adaptive artificial intelligence (AI) models for medical devices, where iterative updates to both models and evaluation datasets complicate performance assessment. We introduce a novel approach with three complementary measurements: learning (model improvement on current data), potential (dataset-driven performance shifts), and retention (knowledge preservation across modification steps), to disentangle performance changes caused by model adaptations versus dynamic environments. Case studies using simulated population shifts demonstrate the approach's utility: gradual transitions enable stable learning and retention, while rapid shifts reveal trade-offs between plasticity and stability. These measurements provide practical insights for regulatory science, enabling rigorous assessment of the safety and effectiveness of adaptive AI systems over sequential modifications.

item.title: LP-GEMM: Integrating Layout Propagation into GEMM Operations
item.content:encoded: In Scientific Computing and modern Machine Learning (ML) workloads, sequences of dependent General Matrix Multiplications (GEMMs) often dominate execution time. While state-of-the-art BLAS libraries aggressively optimize individual GEMM calls, they remain constrained by the BLAS API, which requires each call to independently pack input matrices and restore outputs to a canonical memory layout. In sequential GEMMs, these constraints cause redundant packing and unpacking, wasting valuable computational resources. This paper introduces LP-GEMM, a decomposition of the GEMM kernel that enables packing-layout propagation across sequential GEMM operations. This approach eliminates unnecessary data repacking while preserving full BLAS semantic correctness at the boundaries. We evaluate LP-GEMM on x86 (AVX-512) and RISC-V (RVV 1.0) architectures across MLP-like and Attention-like workloads. Our results show average speedups of 2.25x over OpenBLAS on Intel x86 for sequential GEMMs and competitive gains relative to vendor-optimized libraries such as Intel MKL. We demonstrate the practicality of the approach beyond microbenchmarks by implementing a standalone C++ version of the Llama-3.2 inference path using exclusively BLAS-level GEMM calls. These results confirm that leveraging data layout propagation between operations can significantly boost performance.

item.title: CLEAR: Unlocking Generative Potential for Degraded Image Understanding in Unified Multimodal Models
item.content:encoded: Image degradation from blur, noise, compression, and poor illumination severely undermines multimodal understanding in real-world settings. Unified multimodal models that combine understanding and generation within a single architecture are a natural fit for this challenge, as their generative pathway can model the fine-grained visual structure that degradation destroys. Yet these models fail to leverage their own generative capacity on degraded inputs. We trace this disconnect to two compounding factors: existing training regimes never ask the model to invoke generation during reasoning, and the standard decode-reencode pathway does not support effective joint optimization. We present CLEAR, a framework that connects the two capabilities through three progressive steps: (1) supervised fine-tuning on a degradation-aware dataset to establish the generate-then-answer reasoning pattern; (2) a Latent Representation Bridge that replaces the decode-reencode detour with a direct, optimizable connection between generation and reasoning; (3) Interleaved GRPO, a reinforcement learning method that jointly optimizes text reasoning and visual generation under answer-correctness rewards. We construct MMD-Bench, covering three degradation severity levels across six standard multimodal benchmarks. Experiments show that CLEAR substantially improves robustness on degraded inputs while preserving clean-image performance. Our analysis further reveals that removing pixel-level reconstruction supervision leads to intermediate visual states with higher perceptual quality, suggesting that task-driven optimization and visual quality are naturally aligned.

item.title: Vanast: Virtual Try-On with Human Image Animation via Synthetic Triplet Supervision
item.content:encoded: We present Vanast, a unified framework that generates garment-transferred human animation videos directly from a single human image, garment images, and a pose guidance video. Conventional two-stage pipelines treat image-based virtual try-on and pose-driven animation as separate processes, which often results in identity drift, garment distortion, and front-back inconsistency. Our model addresses these issues by performing the entire process in a single unified step to achieve coherent synthesis. To enable this setting, we construct large-scale triplet supervision. Our data generation pipeline includes generating identity-preserving human images in alternative outfits that differ from garment catalog images, capturing full upper and lower garment triplets to overcome the single-garment-posed video pair limitation, and assembling diverse in-the-wild triplets without requiring garment catalog images. We further introduce a Dual Module architecture for video diffusion transformers to stabilize training, preserve pretrained generative quality, and improve garment accuracy, pose adherence, and identity preservation while supporting zero-shot garment interpolation. Together, these contributions allow Vanast to produce high-fidelity, identity-consistent animation across a wide range of garment types.

item.title: RoboPhD: Evolving Diverse Complex Agents Under Tight Evaluation Budgets
item.content:encoded: 2026 has brought an explosion of interest in LLM-guided evolution of agentic artifacts, with systems like GEPA and Autoresearch demonstrating that LLMs can iteratively improve prompts, code, and agent architectures across diverse domains. As adoption accelerates, a central question emerges: given the same information, the same seed agent, and the same objective, which optimization algorithm yields the best results under the same evaluation budget? This question becomes critical when evaluations are expensive, such as when they require human judgment or multiple LLM calls. We present the first systematic comparison of three optimization paradigms -- Elo tournament selection (RoboPhD), Pareto-based selection (GEPA), and greedy hill-climbing (Autoresearch) -- across four benchmarks spanning abstract reasoning, cloud scheduling, SQL generation, and financial QA, all under a fixed budget of 1,500 evaluations. RoboPhD introduces validation-free evolution: instead of splitting the budget between training and validation, it uses Elo competition on training data to simultaneously evaluate agents and drive evolution. All three systems receive seed agents with diagnostic print() statements that evolution can grow, enabling self-instrumenting agents that develop increasingly informative diagnostics for the benefit of their evolutionary successors. Using a single default configuration, RoboPhD outperforms both GEPA and Autoresearch on three of four benchmarks, losing only on the simplest task, where the winning solution (from our Autoresearch adaptation) required under 90 lines of code. On ARC-AGI, RoboPhD evolves a 22-line seed agent into a 1,013-line multi-strategy system, improving accuracy from 27.8% to 65.8% using Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite as the solver. We release RoboPhD as a versatile toolkit under the MIT license with a simple optimize_anything() API for evolving diverse complex agents.

item.title: Visual Prompt Based Reasoning for Offroad Mapping using Multimodal LLMs
item.content:encoded: Traditional approaches to off-road autonomy rely on separate models for terrain classification, height estimation, and quantifying slip or slope conditions. Utilizing several models requires training each component separately, having task specific datasets, and fine-tuning. In this work, we present a zero-shot approach leveraging SAM2 for environment segmentation and a vision-language model (VLM) to reason about drivable areas. Our approach involves passing to the VLM both the original image and the segmented image annotated with numeric labels for each mask. The VLM is then prompted to identify which regions, represented by these numeric labels, are drivable. Combined with planning and control modules, this unified framework eliminates the need for explicit terrain-specific models and relies instead on the inherent reasoning capabilities of the VLM. Our approach surpasses state-of-the-art trainable models on high resolution segmentation datasets and enables full stack navigation in our Isaac Sim offroad environment.

item.title: Partially deterministic sampling for compressed sensing with denoising guarantees
item.content:encoded: We study compressed sensing when the sampling vectors are chosen from the rows of a unitary matrix. In the literature, these sampling vectors are typically chosen randomly; the use of randomness has enabled major empirical and theoretical advances in the field. However, in practice there are often certain crucial sampling vectors, in which case practitioners will depart from the theory and sample such rows deterministically. In this work, we derive an optimized sampling scheme for Bernoulli selectors which naturally combines random and deterministic selection of rows, thus rigorously deciding which rows should be sampled deterministically. This sampling scheme provides measurable improvements in image compressed sensing for both generative and sparse priors when compared to with-replacement and without-replacement sampling schemes, as we show with theoretical results and numerical experiments. Additionally, our theoretical guarantees feature improved sample complexity bounds compared to previous works, and novel denoising guarantees in this setting.

item.title: Firebolt-VL: Efficient Vision-Language Understanding with Cross-Modality Modulation
item.content:encoded: Recent advances in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have enabled impressive progress in vision-language understanding, yet their high computational cost limits deployment in resource-constrained scenarios such as personal assistants, document understanding, and smart cameras. Most existing methods rely on Transformer-based cross-attention, whose quadratic complexity hinders efficiency. Moreover, small vision-language models often struggle to precisely capture fine-grained, task-relevant visual regions, leading to degraded performance on fine-grained reasoning tasks that limit their effectiveness in the real world. To address these issues, we introduce Firebolt-VL, an efficient vision-language model that replaces the Transformer-based decoder with a Liquid Foundation Model (LFM) decoder. To further enhance visual grounding, we propose a Token-Grid Correlation Module, which computes lightweight correlations between text tokens and image patches and modulates via the state-space model with FiLM conditioning. This enables the model to selectively emphasize visual regions relevant to the textual prompt while maintaining linear-time inference. Experimental results across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that Firebolt-VL achieves accurate, fine-grained understanding with significantly improved efficiency. Our model and code are available at: https://fireboltvl.github.io

item.title: PSY-STEP: Structuring Therapeutic Targets and Action Sequences for Proactive Counseling Dialogue Systems
item.content:encoded: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) aims to identify and restructure automatic negative thoughts pertaining to involuntary interpretations of events, yet existing counseling agents struggle to identify and address them in dialogue settings. To bridge this gap, we introduce STEP, a dataset that models CBT counseling by explicitly reflecting automatic thoughts alongside dynamic, action-level counseling sequences. Using this dataset, we train STEPPER, a counseling agent that proactively elicits automatic thoughts and executes cognitively grounded interventions. To further enhance both decision accuracy and empathic responsiveness, we refine STEPPER through preference learning based on simulated, synthesized counseling sessions. Extensive CBT-aligned evaluations show that STEPPER delivers more clinically grounded, coherent, and personalized counseling compared to other strong baseline models, and achieves higher counselor competence without inducing emotional disruption.

item.title: HorizonWeaver: Generalizable Multi-Level Semantic Editing for Driving Scenes
item.content:encoded: Ensuring safety in autonomous driving requires scalable generation of realistic, controllable driving scenes beyond what real-world testing provides. Yet existing instruction guided image editors, trained on object-centric or artistic data, struggle with dense, safety-critical driving layouts. We propose HorizonWeaver, which tackles three fundamental challenges in driving scene editing: (1) multi-level granularity, requiring coherent object- and scene-level edits in dense environments; (2) rich high-level semantics, preserving diverse objects while following detailed instructions; and (3) ubiquitous domain shifts, handling changes in climate, layout, and traffic across unseen environments. The core of HorizonWeaver is a set of complementary contributions across data, model, and training: (1) Data: Large-scale dataset generation, where we build a paired real/synthetic dataset from Boreas, nuScenes, and Argoverse2 to improve generalization; (2) Model: Language-Guided Masks for fine-grained editing, where semantics-enriched masks and prompts enable precise, language-guided edits; and (3) Training: Content preservation and instruction alignment, where joint losses enforce scene consistency and instruction fidelity. Together, HorizonWeaver provides a scalable framework for photorealistic, instruction-driven editing of complex driving scenes, collecting 255K images across 13 editing categories and outperforming prior methods in L1, CLIP, and DINO metrics, achieving +46.4% user preference and improving BEV segmentation IoU by +33%. Project page: https://msoroco.github.io/horizonweaver/

item.title: ENCRUST: Encapsulated Substitution and Agentic Refinement on a Live Scaffold for Safe C-to-Rust Translation
item.content:encoded: We present Encapsulated Substitution and Agentic Refinement on a Live Scaffold for Safe C-to-Rust Translation, a two-phase pipeline for translating real-world C projects to safe Rust. Existing approaches either produce unsafe output without memory-safety guarantees or translate functions in isolation, failing to detect cross-unit type mismatches or handle unsafe constructs requiring whole-program reasoning. Furthermore, function-level LLM pipelines require coordinated caller updates when type signatures change, while project-scale systems often fail to produce compilable output under real-world dependency complexity. Encrust addresses these limitations by decoupling boundary adaptation from function logic via an Application Binary Interface (ABI)-preserving wrapper pattern and validating each intermediate state against the integrated codebase. Phase 1 (Encapsulated Substitution) translates each function using an ABI-preserving wrapper that splits it into two components: a caller-transparent shim retaining the original raw-pointer signature, and a safe inner function targeted by the LLM with a clean, scope-limited prompt. This enables independent per-function type changes with automatic rollback on failure, without coordinated caller updates. A deterministic, type-directed wrapper elimination pass then removes wrappers after successful translation. Phase 2 (Agentic Refinement) resolves unsafe constructs beyond per-function scope, including static mut globals, skipped wrapper pairs, and failed translations, using an LLM agent operating on the whole codebase under a baseline-aware verification gate. We evaluate Encrust on 7 GNU Coreutils programs and 8 libraries from the Laertes benchmark, showing substantial unsafe-construct reduction across all 15 programs while maintaining full test-vector correctness.

item.title: Temporal Inversion for Learning Interval Change in Chest X-Rays
item.content:encoded: Recent advances in vision--language pretraining have enabled strong medical foundation models, yet most analyze radiographs in isolation, overlooking the key clinical task of comparing prior and current images to assess interval change. For chest radiographs (CXRs), capturing interval change is essential, as radiologists must evaluate not only the static appearance of findings but also how they evolve over time. We introduce TILA (Temporal Inversion-aware Learning and Alignment), a simple yet effective framework that uses temporal inversion, reversing image pairs, as a supervisory signal to enhance the sensitivity of existing temporal vision-language models to directional change. TILA integrates inversion-aware objectives across pretraining, fine-tuning, and inference, complementing conventional appearance modeling with explicit learning of temporal order. We also propose a unified evaluation protocol to assess order sensitivity and consistency under temporal inversion, and introduce MS-CXR-Tretrieval, a retrieval evaluation set constructed through a general protocol that can be applied to any temporal CXR dataset. Experiments on public datasets and real-world hospital cohorts demonstrate that TILA consistently improves progression classification and temporal embedding alignment when applied to multiple existing architectures.

item.title: Anticipatory Reinforcement Learning: From Generative Path-Laws to Distributional Value Functions
item.content:encoded: This paper introduces Anticipatory Reinforcement Learning (ARL), a novel framework designed to bridge the gap between non-Markovian decision processes and classical reinforcement learning architectures, specifically under the constraint of a single observed trajectory. In environments characterised by jump-diffusions and structural breaks, traditional state-based methods often fail to capture the essential path-dependent geometry required for accurate foresight. We resolve this by lifting the state space into a signature-augmented manifold, where the history of the process is embedded as a dynamical coordinate. By utilising a self-consistent field approach, the agent maintains an anticipated proxy of the future path-law, allowing for a deterministic evaluation of expected returns. This transition from stochastic branching to a single-pass linear evaluation significantly reduces computational complexity and variance. We prove that this framework preserves fundamental contraction properties and ensures stable generalisation even in the presence of heavy-tailed noise. Our results demonstrate that by grounding reinforcement learning in the topological features of path-space, agents can achieve proactive risk management and superior policy stability in highly volatile, continuous-time environments.

item.title: 基于功能图的视觉类别连接状态解码研究
item.content:encoded:
通过图神经网络解码视觉类别功能连接状态。



摘要： 本研究利用7T fMRI数据构建了区域级功能图，并训练了一个能够建模正负交互的有符号图神经网络，用于解码特定视觉类别（如运动、食物、车辆）的功能连接状态。该模型揭示了沿腹侧和背侧视觉通路的可重复、生物学有意义的子网络，将分类选择性从体素层面扩展到连接性表征，为机器学习与神经科学的结合提供了新框架。

item.title: DTPTrack：提升视觉跟踪性能的时序抑制模块
item.content:encoded:
DTPTrack通过时序可靠性校准和动态先验合成提升跟踪精度。



摘要： 本文提出DTPTrack，一个轻量且通用的模块，用于抑制多帧跟踪中的模型漂移问题。该框架包含两个核心组件：Temporal Reliability Calibrator（TRC）通过为历史状态分配可靠性分数来过滤噪声，并锚定在真实模板上；Temporal Guidance Synthesizer（TGS）则将校准后的历史信息合成动态时间先验以提供预测指导。DTPTrack被集成到OSTrack、ODTrack和LoRAT等三种跟踪架构中，均取得显著性能提升。基于扩展的LoRATv2骨干网络的最佳模型在LaSOT和GOT-10k基准上分别达到77.5%的Success率和80.3%的AO值，创下新纪录。

item.title: Chat-Scene++：基于多模态大语言模型的3D场景理解框架
item.content:encoded:
Chat-Scene++提升3D场景理解能力，实现精准物体定位与推理。



摘要： 本文提出Chat-Scene++，一个用于3D场景理解的多模态大语言模型框架。该框架通过将3D场景表示为富含上下文的物体序列，实现了以物体为中心的表示与交互。它通过结合大规模预训练的3D和2D编码器提取丰富的物体特征，并支持基于物体的链式推理，无需额外任务头或微调即可在多个3D视觉-语言基准上取得最佳性能。此外，该方法仅需2D输入即可应用于真实场景，避免了复杂的3D重建过程。

item.title: SplatHLoc：基于特征高斯点云的视觉重定位框架
item.content:encoded:
SplatHLoc提升视觉重定位精度与鲁棒性。



摘要： 视觉重定位是3D计算机视觉中的基础任务，用于估计相机在已知场景中再次访问时的位姿。传统基于点的分层方法虽具可扩展性和效率，但受限于图像观测稀疏和特征匹配弱。本文提出SplatHLoc，采用特征高斯点云作为场景表示，并引入自适应视角检索方法，合成更贴近查询视角的虚拟候选，提高初始位姿估计精度。同时，结合高斯渲染特征与图像直接提取特征，在两阶段匹配中分别发挥优势，实现更准确高效的位姿估计。实验表明，SplatHLoc在室内外数据集上均表现出更强的鲁棒性，达到新的先进水平。

item.title: 统一推荐模型缩放架构UniMixer的提出与性能验证
item.content:encoded:
提出统一推荐模型架构UniMixer，提升缩放效率与性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种统一的推荐模型缩放架构UniMixer，旨在提高模型缩放效率并建立统一的理论框架。通过将基于规则的TokenMixer转换为参数化结构，构建了一个可优化的特征混合模块，同时消除了TokenMixer中对头数与token数相等的限制。此外，设计了轻量级的UniMixing-Lite模块，在降低参数和计算成本的同时显著提升模型性能。实验验证了UniMixer在离线与在线场景中的优越缩放能力。

item.title: 政府AI聊天机器人安全防护框架CivicShield研究
item.content:encoded:
CivicShield提升政府AI聊天机器人安全性，有效防御多轮攻击。



摘要： 本文针对政府服务中基于大语言模型的聊天机器人存在的安全漏洞，提出了一种名为CivicShield的跨领域深度防御框架。该框架融合了网络安全、形式化验证、生物免疫系统、航空安全和零信任密码学等理念，包含七层防御机制，包括零信任基础、输入验证、语义防火墙、对话状态机、行为异常检测、多模型共识验证以及分级人工介入。研究构建了涵盖8类多轮攻击的威胁模型，并映射到NIST SP 800-53标准。通过1,436个场景的仿真测试，CivicShield在保持100%检测率的同时，实现了72.9%的综合检测率和2.9%的有效误报率，验证了其在实际部署中的有效性。

item.title: 基于线性注意力架构的可控扩散生成框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出新框架提升线性注意力模型的可控生成性能。



摘要： 本文针对基于扩散模型的可控视觉生成技术进行研究，旨在解决现有方法在多类型条件输入支持和收敛速度方面的不足。作者提出一种适用于线性注意力架构（如SANA）的新型可控扩散框架，核心是一个统一的门控条件模块，通过双路径流水线有效整合多种类型的条件输入。实验表明，该方法在多个任务和基准测试中均取得领先的可控生成效果，显著提升了生成质量与控制能力。

item.title: GameSight：基于知识增强的足球解说生成模型
item.content:encoded:
GameSight提升足球解说准确性与上下文相关性。



摘要： 本文提出GameSight，一个两阶段的足球解说生成模型，通过结合视觉推理和外部历史数据，提高对球员和球队的准确引用，减少上下文错误。实验表明，GameSight在SN-Caption-test-align数据集上的球员对齐准确率提升了18.5%。此外，该模型在段落级准确性和解说质量、比赛级上下文相关性及结构组成方面均优于现有方法，为AI体育应用提供了更丰富、更具互动性的体验。

item.title: ScaleGNN：一种可扩展的图神经网络训练框架
item.content:encoded:
ScaleGNN提升大规模图神经网络训练效率与可扩展性。



摘要： 本文提出ScaleGNN，一种4D并行框架，用于高效的大规模图神经网络（GNN）训练。该框架结合无通信的分布式采样、3D并行矩阵乘法（PMM）和数据并行，显著降低通信开销并提高扩展性。通过统一顶点采样算法，每个GPU可独立构建本地小批量数据，无需进程间通信。此外，还引入了采样与训练重叠、低精度传输、内核融合等优化技术。实验表明，ScaleGNN在多个图数据集上表现优异，尤其在2048 GPU上实现3.5倍的训练速度提升。

item.title: 面向模型的可视化框架与分析研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种模型为中心的可视化框架，用于分析模型行为与效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种以模型为核心的两阶段可视化框架，通过抽象监听器捕捉模型的空间和时间行为，并将其与经典信息可视化流程结合。为实现大规模应用，构建了一个基于检索增强的人类-大语言模型提取工作流，并整理了128篇VIS/VAST模型可视化论文及其331幅编码图表。分析显示，当前模型可视化主要关注结果导向、定量/名义数据类型、统计图表及性能评估。引用加权趋势表明，较少关注模型机制的研究具有较高影响力，但近年来研究较少。该框架可作为比较现有模型可视化系统和指导未来设计的通用方法。

item.title: 相对时差学习的稳定性分析与参数估计研究
item.content:encoded:
相对时差学习在折扣因子接近1时更稳定，且参数估计具有界性。



摘要： 本文研究了相对时差（TD）学习在折扣因子接近1时的稳定性问题。通过引入基线分布，该方法能够改善传统TD方法的收敛速度。文章重点分析了线性函数近似下的相对TD学习算法，并证明当基线分布为状态-动作过程的经验分布时，算法对任意非负基线权重和折扣因子都保持稳定。此外，还进行了参数估计的敏感性分析，揭示了渐近偏差和协方差的特性，表明它们在折扣因子趋近于1时仍保持有界。

item.title: Fast Data在汽车系统工程中的应用与优势
item.content:encoded:
Fast Data提升汽车系统数据质量与效率。



摘要： 随着机器学习模型能力的增强，汽车系统工程对数据质量与相关性的要求不断提高。传统大数据方法依赖大规模数据收集和离线处理，而智能数据方法虽优化了数据选择策略，但仍依赖集中式离线后处理。本文提出Fast Data概念，将数据选择与记录直接置于车辆端，实现基于上下文的实时决策，使数据收集更贴合数据质量目标和策略，从而提升数据的相关性、覆盖关键场景的能力及信息密度，同时减少冗余数据和成本。该方法为现代机器学习算法提供了结构化的数据采集策略基础，支持高效数据获取，推动汽车系统工程中可扩展且经济的机器学习发展。
