item.title: Exploring the Impact of Skin Color on Skin Lesion Segmentation
item.content:encoded: Skin cancer, particularly melanoma, remains a major cause of morbidity and mortality, making early detection critical. AI-driven dermatology systems often rely on skin lesion segmentation as a preprocessing step to delineate the lesion from surrounding skin and support downstream analysis. While fairness concerns regarding skin tone have been widely studied for lesion classification, the influence of skin tone on the segmentation stage remains under-quantified and is frequently assessed using coarse, discrete skin tone categories. In this work, we evaluate three strong segmentation architectures (UNet, DeepLabV3 with a ResNet50 backbone, and DINOv2) on two public dermoscopic datasets (HAM10000 and ISIC2017) and introduce a continuous pigment or contrast analysis that treats pixel-wise ITA values as distributions. Using Wasserstein distances between within-image distributions for skin-only, lesion-only, and whole-image regions, we quantify lesion skin contrast and relate it to segmentation performance across multiple metrics. Within the range represented in these datasets, global skin tone metrics (Fitzpatrick grouping or mean ITA) show weak association with segmentation quality. In contrast, low lesion-skin contrast is consistently associated with larger segmentation errors in models, indicating that boundary ambiguity and low contrast are key drivers of failure. These findings suggest that fairness improvements in dermoscopic segmentation should prioritize robust handling of low-contrast lesions, and the distribution-based pigment measures provide a more informative audit signal than discrete skin-tone categories.

item.title: AGFT: Alignment-Guided Fine-Tuning for Zero-Shot Adversarial Robustness of Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: Pre-trained vision-language models (VLMs) exhibit strong zero-shot generalization but remain vulnerable to adversarial perturbations. Existing classification-guided adversarial fine-tuning methods often disrupt pre-trained cross-modal alignment, weakening visual-textual correspondence and degrading zero-shot performance. In this paper, we propose an Alignment-Guided Fine-Tuning (AGFT) framework that enhances zero-shot adversarial robustness while preserving the cross-modal semantic structure. Unlike label-based methods that rely on hard labels and fail to maintain the relative relationships between image and text, AGFT leverages the probabilistic predictions of the original model for text-guided adversarial training, which aligns adversarial visual features with textual embeddings via soft alignment distributions, improving zero-shot adversarial robustness. To address structural discrepancies introduced by fine-tuning, we introduce a distribution consistency calibration mechanism that adjusts the robust model output to match a temperature-scaled version of the pre-trained model predictions. Extensive experiments across multiple zero-shot benchmarks demonstrate that AGFT outperforms state-of-the-art methods while significantly improving zero-shot adversarial robustness.

item.title: Refined Detection for Gumbel Watermarking
item.content:encoded: We propose a simple detection mechanism for the Gumbel watermarking scheme proposed by Aaronson (2022). The new mechanism is proven to be near-optimal in a problem-dependent sense among all model-agnostic watermarking schemes under the assumption that the next-token distribution is sampled i.i.d.

item.title: Owl-AuraID 1.0: An Intelligent System for Autonomous Scientific Instrumentation and Scientific Data Analysis
item.content:encoded: Scientific discovery increasingly depends on high-throughput characterization, yet automation is hindered by proprietary GUIs and the limited generalizability of existing API-based systems. We present Owl-AuraID, a software-hardware collaborative embodied agent system that adopts a GUI-native paradigm to operate instruments through the same interfaces as human experts. Its skill-centric framework integrates Type-1 (GUI operation) and Type-2 (data analysis) skills into end-to-end workflows, connecting physical sample handling with scientific interpretation. Owl-AuraID demonstrates broad coverage across ten categories of precision instruments and diverse workflows, including multimodal spectral analysis, microscopic imaging, and crystallographic analysis, supporting modalities such as FTIR, NMR, AFM, and TGA. Overall, Owl-AuraID provides a practical, extensible foundation for autonomous laboratories and illustrates a path toward evolving laboratory intelligence through reusable operational and analytical skills. The code are available at https://github.com/OpenOwlab/AuraID.

item.title: Video Models Reason Early: Exploiting Plan Commitment for Maze Solving
item.content:encoded: Video diffusion models exhibit emergent reasoning capabilities like solving mazes and puzzles, yet little is understood about how they reason during generation. We take a first step towards understanding this and study the internal planning dynamics of video models using 2D maze solving as a controlled testbed. Our investigations reveal two findings. Our first finding is early plan commitment: video diffusion models commit to a high-level motion plan within the first few denoising steps, after which further denoising alters visual details but not the underlying trajectory. Our second finding is that path length, not obstacle density, is the dominant predictor of maze difficulty, with a sharp failure threshold at 12 steps. This means video models can only reason over long mazes by chaining together multiple sequential generations. To demonstrate the practical benefits of our findings, we introduce Chaining with Early Planning, or ChEaP, which only spends compute on seeds with promising early plans and chains them together to tackle complex mazes. This improves accuracy from 7% to 67% on long-horizon mazes and by 2.5x overall on hard tasks in Frozen Lake and VR-Bench across Wan2.2-14B and HunyuanVideo-1.5. Our analysis reveals that current video models possess deeper reasoning capabilities than previously recognized, which can be elicited more reliably with better inference-time scaling.

item.title: Learning Diagnostic Reasoning for Decision Support in Toxicology
item.content:encoded: Acute poly-substance intoxication requires rapid, life-saving decisions under substantial uncertainty, as clinicians must rely on incomplete ingestion details and nonspecific symptoms. Effective diagnostic reasoning in this chaotic environment requires fusing unstructured, non-medical narratives (e.g. paramedic scene descriptions and unreliable patient self-reports or known histories), with structured medical data like vital signs. While Large Language Models (LLMs) show potential for processing such heterogeneous inputs, they struggle in this setting, often underperforming simple baselines that rely solely on patient histories. To address this, we present DeToxR (Decision-support for Toxicology with Reasoning), the first adaptation of Reinforcement Learning (RL) to emergency toxicology. We design a robust data-fusion engine for multi-label prediction across 14 substance classes based on an LLM finetuned with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO). We optimize the model's reasoning directly using a clinical performance reward. By formulating a multi-label agreement metric as the reward signal, the model is explicitly penalized for missing co-ingested substances and hallucinating absent poisons. Our model significantly outperforms its unadapted base LLM counterpart and supervised baselines. Furthermore, in a clinical validation study, the model indicates a clinical advantage by outperforming an expert toxicologist in identifying the correct poisons (Micro-F1: 0.644 vs. 0.473). These results demonstrate the potential of RL-aligned LLMs to synthesize unstructured pre-clinical narratives and structured medical data for decision support in high-stakes environments.

item.title: Developing a Guideline for the Labovian-Structural Analysis of Oral Narratives in Japanese
item.content:encoded: Narrative analysis is a cornerstone of qualitative research. One leading approach is the Labovian model, but its application is labor-intensive, requiring a holistic, recursive interpretive process that moves back and forth between individual parts of the transcript and the transcript as a whole. Existing Labovian datasets are available only in English, which differs markedly from Japanese in terms of grammar and discourse conventions. To address this gap, we introduce the first systematic guidelines for Labovian narrative analysis of Japanese narrative data. Our guidelines retain all six Labovian categories and extend the framework by providing explicit rules for clause segmentation tailored to Japanese constructions. In addition, our guidelines cover a broader range of clause types and narrative types. Using these guidelines, annotators achieved high agreement in clause segmentation (Fleiss' kappa = 0.80) and moderate agreement in two structural classification tasks (Krippendorff's alpha = 0.41 and 0.45, respectively), one of which is slightly higher than that found in prior work despite the use of finer-grained distinctions. This paper describes the Labovian model, the proposed guidelines, the annotation process, and their utility. It concludes by discussing the challenges encountered during the annotation process and the prospects for developing a larger dataset for structural narrative analysis in Japanese qualitative research.

item.title: AEC-Bench: A Multimodal Benchmark for Agentic Systems in Architecture, Engineering, and Construction
item.content:encoded: The AEC-Bench is a multimodal benchmark for evaluating agentic systems on real-world tasks in the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AEC) domain. The benchmark covers tasks requiring drawing understanding, cross-sheet reasoning, and construction project-level coordination. This report describes the benchmark motivation, dataset taxonomy, evaluation protocol, and baseline results across several domain-specific foundation model harnesses. We use AEC-Bench to identify consistent tools and harness design techniques that uniformly improve performance across foundation models in their own base harnesses, such as Claude Code and Codex. We openly release our benchmark dataset, agent harness, and evaluation code for full replicability at https://github.com/nomic-ai/aec-bench under an Apache 2 license.

item.title: Model Predictive Path Integral PID Control for Learning-Based Path Following
item.content:encoded: Classical proportional--integral--derivative (PID) control is widely employed in industrial applications; however, achieving higher performance often motivates the adoption of model predictive control (MPC). Although gradient-based methods are the standard for real-time optimization, sampling-based approaches have recently gained attention. In particular, model predictive path integral (MPPI) control enables gradient-free optimization and accommodates non-differentiable models and objective functions. However, directly sampling control input sequences may yield discontinuous inputs and increase the optimization dimensionality in proportion to the prediction horizon. This study proposes MPPI--PID control, which applies MPPI to optimize PID gains at each control step, thereby replacing direct high-dimensional input-sequence optimization with low-dimensional gain-space optimization. This formulation enhances sample efficiency and yields smoother inputs via the PID structure. We also provide theoretical insights, including an information-theoretic interpretation that unifies MPPI and MPPI--PID, an analysis of the effect of optimization dimensionality on sample efficiency, and a characterization of input continuity induced by the PID structure. The proposed method is evaluated on the learning-based path following of a mini forklift using a residual-learning dynamics model that integrates a physical model with a neural network. System identification is performed with real driving data. Numerical path-following experiments demonstrate that MPPI--PID improves tracking performance compared with fixed-gain PID and achieves performance comparable to conventional MPPI while significantly reducing input increments. Furthermore, the proposed method maintains favorable performance even with substantially fewer samples, demonstrating its improved sample efficiency.

item.title: View-oriented Conversation Compiler for Agent Trace Analysis
item.content:encoded: Agent traces carry increasing analytical value in the era of context learning and harness-driven agentic cognition, yet most prior work treats conversation format as a trivial engineering detail. Modern agent conversations contain deeply structured content, including nested tool calls and results, chain-of-thought reasoning blocks, sub-agent invocations, context-window compaction boundaries, and harness-injected system directives, whose complexity far exceeds that of simple user-assistant exchanges. Feeding such traces to a reflector or other analytical mechanism in plain text, JSON, YAML, or via grep can materially degrade analysis quality. This paper presents VCC (View-oriented Conversation Compiler), a compiler (lex, parse, IR, lower, emit) that transforms raw agent JSONL logs into a family of structured views: a full view (lossless transcript serving as the canonical line-number coordinate system), a user-interface view (reconstructing the interaction as the user actually perceived it), and an adaptive view (a structure-preserving projection governed by a relevance predicate). In a context-learning experiment on AppWorld, replacing only the reflector's input format, from raw JSONL to VCC-compiled views, leads to higher pass rates across all three model configurations tested, while cutting reflector token consumption by half to two-thirds and producing more concise learned memory. These results suggest that message format functions as infrastructure for context learning, not as an incidental implementation choice.

item.title: Predictor-Based Output-Feedback Control of Linear Systems with Time-Varying Input and Measurement Delays via Neural-Approximated Prediction Horizons
item.content:encoded: Due to simplicity and strong stability guarantees, predictor feedback methods have stood as a popular approach for time delay systems since the 1950s. For time-varying delays, however, implementation requires computing a prediction horizon defined by the inverse of the delay function, which is rarely available in closed form and must be approximated. In this work, we formulate the inverse delay mapping as an operator learning problem and study predictor feedback under approximation of the prediction horizon. We propose two approaches: (i) a numerical method based on time integration of an equivalent ODE, and (ii) a data-driven method using neural operators to learn the inverse mapping. We show that both approaches achieve arbitrary approximation accuracy over compact sets, with complementary trade-offs in computational cost and scalability. Building on these approximations, we then develop an output-feedback predictor design for systems with delays in both the input and the measurement. We prove that the resulting closed-loop system is globally exponentially stable when the prediction horizon is approximated with sufficiently small error. Lastly, numerical experiments validate the proposed methods and illustrate their trade-offs between accuracy and computational efficiency.

item.title: ASI-Evolve: AI Accelerates AI
item.content:encoded: Can AI accelerate the development of AI itself? While recent agentic systems have shown strong performance on well-scoped tasks with rapid feedback, it remains unclear whether they can tackle the costly, long-horizon, and weakly supervised research loops that drive real AI progress. We present ASI-Evolve, an agentic framework for AI-for-AI research that closes this loop through a learn-design-experiment-analyze cycle. ASI-Evolve augments standard evolutionary agents with two key components: a cognition base that injects accumulated human priors into each round of exploration, and a dedicated analyzer that distills complex experimental outcomes into reusable insights for future iterations. To our knowledge, ASI-Evolve is the first unified framework to demonstrate AI-driven discovery across three central components of AI development: data, architectures, and learning algorithms. In neural architecture design, it discovered 105 SOTA linear attention architectures, with the best discovered model surpassing DeltaNet by +0.97 points, nearly 3x the gain of recent human-designed improvements. In pretraining data curation, the evolved pipeline improves average benchmark performance by +3.96 points, with gains exceeding 18 points on MMLU. In reinforcement learning algorithm design, discovered algorithms outperform GRPO by up to +12.5 points on AMC32, +11.67 points on AIME24, and +5.04 points on OlympiadBench. We further provide initial evidence that this AI-for-AI paradigm can transfer beyond the AI stack through experiments in mathematics and biomedicine. Together, these results suggest that ASI-Evolve represents a promising step toward enabling AI to accelerate AI across the foundational stages of development, offering early evidence for the feasibility of closed-loop AI research.

item.title: Retinal Malady Classification using AI: A novel ViT-SVM combination architecture
item.content:encoded: Macular Holes, Central serous retinopathy and Diabetic Retinopathy are one of the most widespread maladies of the eyes responsible for either partial or complete vision loss, thus making it clear that early detection of the mentioned defects is detrimental for the well-being of the patient. This study intends to introduce the application of Vision Transformer and Support Vector Machine based hybrid architecture (ViT-SVM) and analyse its performance to classify the optical coherence topography (OCT) Scans with the intention to automate the early detection of these retinal defects.

item.title: Dual-Imbalance Continual Learning for Real-World Food Recognition
item.content:encoded: Visual food recognition in real-world dietary logging scenarios naturally exhibits severe data imbalance, where a small number of food categories appear frequently while many others occur rarely, resulting in long-tailed class distributions. In practice, food recognition systems often operate in a continual learning setting, where new categories are introduced sequentially over time. However, existing studies typically assume that each incremental step introduces a similar number of new food classes, which rarely happens in real world where the number of newly observed categories can vary significantly across steps, leading to highly uneven learning dynamics. As a result, continual food recognition exhibits a dual imbalance: imbalanced samples within each food class and imbalanced numbers of new food classes to learn at each incremental learning step. In this work, we introduce DIME, a Dual-Imbalance-aware Adapter Merging framework for continual food recognition. DIME learns lightweight adapters for each task using parameter-efficient fine-tuning and progressively integrates them through a class-count guided spectral merging strategy. A rank-wise threshold modulation mechanism further stabilizes the merging process by preserving dominant knowledge while allowing adaptive updates. The resulting model maintains a single merged adapter for inference, enabling efficient deployment without accumulating task-specific modules. Experiments on realistic long-tailed food benchmarks under our step-imbalanced setup show that the proposed method consistently improves by more than 3% over the strongest existing continual learning baselines. Code is available at https://github.com/xiaoyanzhang1/DIME.

item.title: Scalable AI-assisted Workflow Management for Detector Design Optimization Using Distributed Computing
item.content:encoded: The Production and Distributed Analysis (PanDA) system, originally developed for the ATLAS experiment at the CERN Large Hadron Collider (LHC), has evolved into a robust platform for orchestrating large-scale workflows across distributed computing resources. Coupled with its intelligent Distributed Dispatch and Scheduling (iDDS) component, PanDA supports AI/ML-driven workflows through a scalable and flexible workflow engine. We present an AI-assisted framework for detector design optimization that integrates multi-objective Bayesian optimization with the PanDA--iDDS workflow engine to coordinate iterative simulations across heterogeneous resources. The framework addresses the challenge of exploring high-dimensional parameter spaces inherent in modern detector design. We demonstrate the framework using benchmark problems and realistic studies of the ePIC and dRICH detectors for the Electron-Ion Collider (EIC). Results show improved automation, scalability, and efficiency in multi-objective optimization. This work establishes a flexible and extensible paradigm for AI-driven detector design and other computationally intensive scientific applications.

item.title: Towards Empowering Consumers through Sentence-level Readability Scoring in German ESG Reports
item.content:encoded: With the ever-growing urgency of sustainability in the economy and society, and the massive stream of information that comes with it, consumers need reliable access to that information. To address this need, companies began publishing so called Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) reports, both voluntarily and forced by law. To serve the public, these reports must be addressed not only to financial experts but also to non-expert audiences. But are they written clearly enough? In this work, we extend an existing sentence-level dataset of German ESG reports with crowdsourced readability annotations. We find that, in general, native speakers perceive sentences in ESG reports as easy to read, but also that readability is subjective. We apply various readability scoring methods and evaluate them regarding their prediction error and correlation with human rankings. Our analysis shows that, while LLM prompting has potential for distinguishing clear from hard-to-read sentences, a small finetuned transformer predicts human readability with the lowest error. Averaging predictions of multiple models can slightly improve the performance at the cost of slower inference.

item.title: Deep Learning-Assisted Improved Differential Fault Attacks on Lightweight Stream Ciphers
item.content:encoded: Lightweight cryptographic primitives are widely deployed in resource-constraint environment, particularly in the Internet of Things (IoT) devices. Due to their public accessibility, these devices are vulnerable to physical attacks, especially fault attacks. Recently, deep learning-based cryptanalytic techniques have demonstrated promising results; however, their application to fault attacks remains limited, particularly for stream ciphers. In this work, we investigate the feasibility of deep learning assisted differential fault attack on three lightweight stream ciphers, namely ACORNv3, MORUSv2 and ATOM, under a relaxed fault model, where a single-bit bit-flipping fault is injected at an unknown location. We train multilayer perceptron (MLP) models to identify the fault locations. Experimental results show that the trained models achieve high identification accuracies of 0.999880, 0.999231 and 0.823568 for ACORNv3, MORUSv2 and ATOM, respectively, and outperform traditional signature-based methods. For the secret recovery process, we introduce a threshold-based method to optimize the number of fault injections required to recover the secret information. The results show that the initial state of ACORN can be recovered with 21 to 34 faults; while MORUS requires 213 to 248 faults, with at most 6 bits of guessing. Both attacks reduce the attack complexity compared to existing works. For ATOM, the results show that it possesses a higher security margin, as majority of state bits in the Non-linear Feedback Shift Register (NFSR) can only be recovered under a precise control model. To the best of our knowledge, this work provides the first experimental results of differential fault attacks on ATOM.

item.title: Nomad: Autonomous Exploration and Discovery
item.content:encoded: We introduce Nomad, a system for autonomous data exploration and insight discovery. Given a corpus of documents, databases, or other data sources, users rarely know the full set of questions, hypotheses, or connections that could be explored. As a result, query-driven question answering and prompt-driven deep-research systems remain limited by human framing and often fail to cover the broader insight space. Nomad addresses this problem with an exploration-first architecture. It constructs an explicit Exploration Map over the domain and systematically traverses it to balance breadth and depth. It generates and selects hypotheses and investigates them with an explorer agent that can use document search, web search, and database tools. Candidate insights are then checked by an independent verifier before entering a reporting pipeline that produces cited reports and higher-level meta-reports. We also present a comprehensive evaluation framework for autonomous discovery systems that measures trustworthiness, report quality, and diversity. Using a corpus of selected UN and WHO reports, we show that \nomad{} produces more trustworthy and higher-quality reports than baselines, while also producing more diverse insights over several runs. Nomad is a step toward autonomous systems that not only answer user questions or conduct directed research, but also discover which questions, research directions, and insights are worth surfacing in the first place.

item.title: Automatic Identification of Parallelizable Loops Using Transformer-Based Source Code Representations
item.content:encoded: Automatic parallelization remains a challenging problem in software engineering, particularly in identifying code regions where loops can be safely executed in parallel on modern multi-core architectures. Traditional static analysis techniques, such as dependence analysis and polyhedral models, often struggle with irregular or dynamically structured code. In this work, we propose a Transformer-based approach to classify the parallelization potential of source code, focusing on distinguishing independent (parallelizable) loops from undefined ones. We adopt DistilBERT to process source code sequences using subword tokenization, enabling the model to capture contextual syntactic and semantic patterns without handcrafted features. The approach is evaluated on a balanced dataset combining synthetically generated loops and manually annotated real-world code, using 10-fold cross-validation and multiple performance metrics. Results show consistently high performance, with mean accuracy above 99\% and low false positive rates, demonstrating robustness and reliability. Compared to prior token-based methods, the proposed approach simplifies preprocessing while improving generalization and maintaining computational efficiency. These findings highlight the potential of lightweight Transformer models for practical identification of parallelization opportunities at the loop level.

item.title: Bringing Up a Bilingual BabyLM: Investigating Multilingual Language Acquisition Using Small-Scale Models
item.content:encoded: Multilingualism is incredibly common around the world, leading to many important theoretical and practical questions about how children learn multiple languages at once. For example, does multilingual acquisition lead to delays in learning? Are there better and worse ways to structure multilingual input? Many correlational studies address these questions, but it is surprisingly difficult to get definitive answers because children cannot be randomly assigned to be multilingual and data are typically not matched between languages. We use language model training as a method for simulating a variety of highly controlled exposure conditions, and create matched 100M-word mono- and bilingual datasets using synthetic data and machine translation. We train GPT-2 models on monolingual and bilingual data organized to reflect a range of exposure regimes, and evaluate their performance on perplexity, grammaticality, and semantic knowledge. Across model scales and measures, bilingual models perform similarly to monolingual models in one language, but show strong performance in the second language as well. These results suggest that there are no strong differences between different bilingual exposure regimes, and that bilingual input poses no in-principle challenges for agnostic statistical learners.

item.title: 基于复数投影与抗碰撞损失的方面情感分析方法
item.content:encoded:
提出新框架解决情感与方面语义混淆问题。



摘要： 本文针对方面情感分析中的表示纠缠问题，提出一种基于零初始化残差复数投影和抗碰撞掩码角度损失的新框架。该方法将文本特征映射到复数语义空间，利用相位解耦情感极性，同时通过振幅编码主观描述的语义强度和词汇丰富性。为解决碰撞瓶颈，引入抗碰撞掩码以增强极性内一致性并扩大极性间区分度。实验表明该框架在Macro-F1指标上达到0.8851的最优表现，几何分析进一步验证了无约束振幅和相位驱动目标的重要性。

item.title: 通过进化框架发现新型强化学习算法
item.content:encoded:
进化框架用于发现新型强化学习算法，性能媲美现有基准。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于进化的强化学习算法发现框架，直接搜索可执行的更新规则以实现完整的训练过程。该方法基于REvolve系统，利用大语言模型作为生成变异算子，从奖励函数发现扩展到算法发现。为促进非标准学习规则的出现，搜索过程中排除了如Actor-Critic结构、时序差分损失等传统机制。由于强化学习算法对内部标量参数高度敏感，研究引入了一个进化后精炼阶段，由大语言模型为每个演化出的更新规则提出可行的超参数范围。在多个Gymnasium基准上进行端到端评估，所发现的算法性能与SAC、PPO、DQN和A2C等现有基准相当。

item.title: PhysHDR-GS：一种基于物理启发的高动态范围新视角合成框架
item.content:encoded:
PhysHDR-GS提升HDR新视角合成效果，实现更真实场景重建。



摘要： 本文提出PhysHDR-GS，一种基于物理启发的高动态范围新视角合成（HDR-NVS）框架。该方法通过建模场景的固有反射率和可调环境光照，解决了传统方法在捕捉光照依赖外观方面的不足。PhysHDR-GS采用互补图像曝光分支和高斯光照分支，分别精确再现标准相机观测和光照变化。训练过程中引入跨分支HDR一致性损失和光照引导梯度缩放策略，有效解决梯度不足问题。实验表明，该方法在真实和合成数据集上均取得显著性能提升，PSNR提高2.04 dB，同时保持实时渲染速度（最高76 FPS）。

item.title: 基于狗斗行为的新型元启发式算法Dogfight Search
item.content:encoded:
Dogfight Search算法在优化问题中表现优异，超越多种先进算法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Dogfight Search（DoS）的新型元启发式算法，灵感来源于战斗机之间的战术协作行为，但其搜索机制基于运动学中的位移积分方程构建。通过在CEC2017、CEC2022基准测试函数、10个实际约束优化问题以及山地路径规划任务中的实验验证，DoS在整体性能上显著优于7种先进算法，并在Friedman排名中位列第一。此外，与3种最先进的算法相比，DoS仍保持领先优势，展现出强大的竞争力。相关源代码已公开。

item.title: 基于中观层面模拟的生成代理系统研究
item.content:encoded:
提出中观层面模拟方法，提升群体行为分析与隐私保护能力。



摘要： 本文探讨了生成代理模拟的三种尺度：个体角色交互、群体行为分析以及中观层面的群体表示。作者提出Synonymix框架，通过图结构抽象和合并多个生活故事角色，构建可查询的集体表示，用于分析或生成合成角色。实验表明，该方法在保留行为信号方面优于传统人口统计基准，并具备良好的隐私保障。文章还讨论了中观模拟带来的交互方式变化及高保真角色是否能真实反映生活体验的问题。

item.title: D2Skill：一种动态双粒度技能库提升智能体强化学习性能
item.content:encoded:
D2Skill通过双粒度技能建模提升智能体强化学习效果。



摘要： 本文提出D2Skill，一种用于智能体强化学习的动态双粒度技能库。该方法将可复用经验组织为任务技能和步骤技能，分别提供高层指导和细粒度决策支持。通过对比基线与技能注入的滚动结果，D2Skill利用性能差距生成回顾性效用信号，用于技能更新和策略优化。实验表明，在ALFWorld和WebShop任务中，D2Skill相比无技能基线提升了10-20个百分点的成功率，且所学技能具有高效用、跨场景迁移能力，训练开销较小。

item.title: GhostWriteBench与TRACE：用于LLM作者归属的基准与指纹方法
item.content:encoded:
提出GhostWriteBench数据集和TRACE方法，提升LLM作者归属性能。



摘要： 本文介绍了GhostWriteBench，一个用于大语言模型（LLM）作者归属的长文本数据集，包含每本书50K+字的生成文本，旨在测试跨分布外（OOD）场景下的泛化能力。同时，作者提出了TRACE，一种可解释且轻量的指纹方法，适用于开源和闭源模型。TRACE通过捕捉标记级的转移模式（如词频排名）来生成指纹，实验表明其在GhostWriteBench上表现优异，具有良好的鲁棒性和在有限训练数据下的适用性。

item.title: Drift-AR：通过熵信号联合加速自回归与扩散模型
item.content:encoded:
Drift-AR利用熵信号同时提升自回归和扩散模型的生成速度。



摘要： 本文提出Drift-AR，一种结合自回归（AR）与扩散模型的混合框架，通过引入预测熵作为统一信号，同时优化两个阶段的生成效率。在AR阶段，采用熵引导的推测解码以减少草案拒绝；在视觉解码阶段，将熵重新解释为物理方差，实现单步解码。该方法无需额外计算成本，实验表明其在多个基准上实现了3.8到5.5倍的速度提升，且保持高质量生成。

item.title: 基于即时导数的循环网络在线适应方法
item.content:encoded:
循环网络可通过即时导数实现在线自适应，无需雅可比传播。



摘要： 本文提出一种新的循环神经网络在线学习方法，表明不需要雅可比传播即可实现网络的在线适应。隐藏状态在前向传递中已携带时间信用信息，只要避免使用过时的追踪记忆并归一化梯度尺度，即可通过即时导数进行有效训练。研究还提出了一条架构规则：当梯度必须通过没有输出旁路的非线性状态更新时，需要使用 {eta}2 归一化，否则无需。实验结果表明，在十个架构、真实灵长类神经数据和流式机器学习基准测试中，使用 RMSprop 的即时导数方法在内存消耗减少 1000 倍的情况下，性能可与完整 RTRL 相媲美，甚至更优，支持到 n = 1024 的规模。

item.title: Stepwise-Flow-GRPO改进扩散模型的信用分配方法
item.content:encoded:
提出Stepwise-Flow-GRPO改进扩散生成中的信用分配策略。



摘要： 本文针对Flow-GRPO在扩散生成过程中采用统一信用分配的问题，提出Stepwise-Flow-GRPO方法。该方法根据每一步的奖励提升进行信用分配，利用Tweedie公式获取中间奖励估计，并引入基于增益的优势函数，提高了样本效率和收敛速度。同时，引入类似DDIM的SDE，在保持随机性的同时提升奖励质量。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的动态中间件SAGAI-MID解决分布式系统中的模式不匹配问题
item.content:encoded:
SAGAI-MID利用LLM动态处理分布式系统中的模式不匹配问题。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为SAGAI-MID的FastAPI中间件，旨在解决现代分布式系统中由于服务异构性导致的模式不匹配问题。该系统通过五层管道架构，结合结构差异分析与LLM语义分析，实现运行时的模式检测与修复。采用两种解析策略：按请求进行LLM转换和生成可复用适配器代码，并通过三层安全机制确保可靠性。实验评估显示，在10个互操作场景中，最佳配置达到0.90的pass@1准确率，其中CODEGEN策略优于DIRECT策略，且不同模型在成本与性能之间存在显著差异。

item.title: 4DSurf：一种用于动态场景表面重建的统一框架
item.content:encoded:
提出4DSurf框架，提升动态场景表面重建精度与一致性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为4DSurf的新型统一框架，用于通用动态场景表面重建。该方法无需预先指定场景中的物体数量或类型，能够处理大范围的表面变形和时间不一致性。核心创新在于引入了由有符号距离函数流正则化驱动的高斯变形，以约束高斯点的运动与表面演变保持一致。此外，通过重叠段划分策略，将序列分割为小变形的重叠片段，并在片段间传递几何信息。实验结果表明，该方法在Hi4D和CMU Panoptic数据集上分别比现有最佳方法提升了49%和19%的Chamfer距离性能，并在稀疏视角条件下表现出更优的时间一致性。

item.title: ITQ3_S：一种基于FWHT的高效3位量化方法
item.content:encoded:
ITQ3_S通过FWHT提升3位量化精度与性能。



摘要： 本文提出ITQ3_S，一种针对大语言模型的3位权重量化方法，结合TurboQuant策略和快速沃尔什-哈达玛变换（FWHT），有效解决传统3位量化因权重分布不均导致的精度损失问题。通过预旋转权重空间并进行均匀三元编码，ITQ3_S实现了接近高斯分布的权重分布，提升了量化效果。同时，该方法在NVIDIA RTX 5090上实现了与FP16相当的困惑度，并且吞吐量超过4位量化方案的1.5倍，证明了其在消费级硬件上的实用性与高效性。

item.title: ForestSim：用于森林环境智能车辆的高保真合成数据集
item.content:encoded:
ForestSim为森林环境下的智能车辆提供高保真合成数据集。



摘要： 本文介绍了ForestSim，一个用于训练和评估智能车辆在森林等非结构化环境中进行语义分割的高保真合成数据集。该数据集包含2094张逼真的图像，覆盖25种不同环境，涵盖多种季节、地形和植被密度。通过整合Unreal Engine和Microsoft AirSim，生成了20个与自主导航相关的像素级标签。尽管面对非结构化场景的挑战，ForestSim仍展现出良好的性能，为下一代智能越野车辆的感知研究提供了可扩展和可访问的基础。数据集和代码已公开。

item.title: EventTrack6D：基于事件相机的6D物体位姿跟踪框架
item.content:encoded:
EventTrack6D实现快速、精准的6D物体位姿跟踪，适用于动态场景。



摘要： EventTrack6D是一种基于事件相机的6D物体位姿跟踪框架，能够在高速动态场景中有效工作。该方法通过重建任意时间点的强度和深度信息，无需针对特定物体进行训练即可泛化到新物体。它利用最近的深度测量进行双重建，从稀疏事件流中恢复密集的光度和几何线索，支持超过120 FPS的实时运行，并在快速运动中保持时间一致性。研究还引入了一个大规模合成数据集和两个互补评估集，用于训练和验证。模型仅在合成数据上训练，即可有效泛化到真实场景，无需微调，展示了事件相机在6D位姿跟踪中的有效性。

item.title: 大型语言模型在从应用商店评论生成用户故事中的应用
item.content:encoded:
LLMs能有效生成可用用户故事，但独立性仍不足。



摘要： 本文研究了GPT-3.5 Turbo、Gemini 2.0 Flash和Mistral 7B Instruct等大型语言模型（LLMs）在从应用商店评论中生成可用用户故事的能力。通过Mini-BAR数据集测试了零次、一次和两次提示方法的效果，并使用RUST框架和RoBERTa分类器评估生成结果的质量。结果显示，LLMs在生成流畅、格式良好的用户故事方面表现优异，甚至可超越人类，但在生成独立且独特的用户故事方面仍有不足。研究证明LLMs能够将非结构化的用户反馈转化为可操作的软件需求，为开发提供清晰指导。

item.title: 基于LRAN的流体控制强化学习研究
item.content:encoded:
使用LRAN提升2D Rayleigh-Bénard对流的RL控制效率。



摘要： 本文研究了利用线性循环自编码网络（LRAN）加速二维Rayleigh-Bénard对流的强化学习控制。通过两种训练策略进行评估：一种是基于随机动作生成的预计算数据的代理模型，另一种是基于不断演进策略的数据迭代训练。结果表明，仅使用代理模型会降低控制性能，但将代理模型与直接数值模拟结合的预训练方案可恢复最先进的性能，并减少40%以上的训练时间。此外，策略感知的训练方式有效缓解了分布偏移问题，提高了策略相关状态空间中的预测准确性。

item.title: ParaSpeechCLAP：多模态语音与文本风格嵌入模型
item.content:encoded:
ParaSpeechCLAP提升语音与文本风格映射性能。



摘要： 本文提出ParaSpeechCLAP，一个双编码器对比模型，能够将语音和文本风格描述符映射到统一的嵌入空间中，支持更广泛的说话人级和话语级特征（如音调、质感和情绪）。研究构建了专用的ParaSpeechCLAP-Intrinsic和ParaSpeechCLAP-Situational模型以及统一的ParaSpeechCLAP-Combined模型，发现专业化模型在单一风格维度上表现更强，而统一模型在组合评估中表现更优。此外，ParaSpeechCLAP-Intrinsic通过额外分类损失和类别平衡训练获得提升。实验表明，该模型在风格描述检索、语音属性分类及作为推理时奖励模型方面均优于基线模型。

item.title: LLM API调用的信息流分析与边界突破
item.content:encoded:
提出首个信息流方法跨越NL/PL边界，提升数据追踪能力。



摘要： 文章探讨了LLM API调用在程序分析中的挑战，指出其导致的数据流分析失效问题。作者提出了基于定量信息流理论的24种标签分类体系，用于描述LLM输出的信息保留程度和输出形式。通过标注9,083个真实Python文件中的占位符输出对，验证了分类的可靠性。该方法在污点传播和反向切片等应用中表现出色，有效提升了数据追踪的准确性，并为工具开发提供了过滤标准。

item.title: 基于TensorRT的Transformer模型GPU加速推理管道设计与评估
item.content:encoded:
GPU加速推理提升Transformer模型性能，保持高精度与低延迟。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于NVIDIA TensorRT的GPU加速推理管道，用于优化Transformer模型。通过混合精度策略，对BERT-base和GPT-2进行测试，结果显示系统在CPU基线上提升了64.4倍，单样本推理延迟低于10毫秒，内存使用减少63%。该方法在保持FP32精度的关键操作（如softmax和层归一化）的同时，采用FP16优化线性层，确保数值稳定性与高相似度。系统模块化、容器化，支持360多种配置的可重复基准测试，并在多GPU环境下验证了其一致性与鲁棒性。下游任务如SST-2和WikiText-2的实验表明，混合精度策略不影响模型准确率，适用于对延迟敏感的应用场景。

item.title: 面向主观性敏感模型的评估标准研究
item.content:encoded:
探讨主观性模型评估标准，发现当前研究存在不足。



摘要： 本文讨论了主观性在自然语言处理中的重要性，并指出当前模型评估方法可能未能充分反映其多样性。作者提出了七个评估标准，旨在更准确地衡量主观性敏感模型的表现。通过分析60篇论文的实验设计，发现目前对主观性表达、输入类型区分以及评估标准之间的互动等方面研究仍不足。该研究强调了用户视角在模型评估中的关键作用，并呼吁改进评估方法以更好地支持多元观点。

item.title: JaWildText：评估日语文本理解的基准测试
item.content:encoded:
引入JaWildText，评估视觉语言模型在日语文本理解上的能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了JaWildText，这是一个针对日语文本理解的诊断基准测试，旨在填补现有多语言基准在日语场景文本理解方面的不足。该数据集包含3,241个实例，涵盖2,961张日本实地拍摄的图像，共计112万标注字符，涉及3,643种独特字符类型。JaWildText包含三项互补任务：密集场景文本问答、收据关键信息提取和手写OCR，用于评估不同视觉组织、输出格式和书写风格下的文本理解能力。实验表明，最佳模型在三个任务上的平均得分为0.64，识别仍是主要瓶颈，尤其是汉字。该数据集将公开并提供评估代码。

item.title: EpiPersona：通过显式人格与情境耦合提升大语言模型的多样性适应能力
item.content:encoded:
EpiPersona框架提升LLM对多样化偏好的适应性。



摘要： 本文提出EpiPersona框架，用于解决大语言模型在适应个体和少数群体偏好时，因混合稳定人格特征与情境因素而导致泛化能力受限的问题。该框架通过将噪声偏好反馈投影到低维人格空间，聚合相似人格为离散编码，从而分离持久人格特征与情境信号，无需预定义偏好维度。随后，人格表示与当前情境耦合，实现情境感知的偏好预测。实验表明，EpiPersona在多种场景下均优于基线模型，尤其在困难的情境转移任务中表现突出，且在稀疏偏好数据下仍具有效性。

item.title: 基于概率单纯形的去噪生成框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于概率单纯形的去噪生成方法，提升离散结构建模效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为 simplex denoising 的生成框架，该框架在概率单纯形上进行操作，而非直接在离散状态空间中。其核心思想是采用非马尔可夫的噪声生成方式，使不同时间点的噪声表示条件独立。这种方法在保持去噪生成模型理论保证的同时，消除了不必要的约束，提升了性能并简化了模型设计。实验表明，该方法在合成和真实世界的图基准数据集上均优于现有的离散扩散和流匹配方法，验证了概率单纯形在离散生成建模中的有效性。

item.title: 自动选择原型提升强化学习可解释性
item.content:encoded:
提出一种自动选择原型的方法提升RL可解释性。



摘要： 近年来，强化学习（RL）在多个领域得到广泛应用，但随着模型复杂度的增加，其可解释性面临挑战。尽管已有许多解释方法用于计算机视觉和自然语言处理，但在RL中的应用仍有限。Prototype-Wrapper Networks (PW-Nets) 在提升RL可解释性方面表现出色，但需要手动定义参考原型，依赖专家知识。本文提出一种自动选择最优原型的方法，无需人工干预，在标准Gym环境中实验表明，该方法性能与现有PW-Nets相当，并保持与原始黑盒模型的竞争性。

item.title: 基于分布式卷积神经网络的多尺度目标检测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于DisCNN的多尺度目标检测方法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于分布式卷积神经网络（DisCNN）的简单目标检测方法。该方法利用DisCNN输出向量在特定正类上的单调性，通过识别所有高得分区域并重叠形成边界框来检测目标。检测过程基于多尺度特征，从具体子特征到抽象特征进行识别。训练只需以目标为中心的图像数据和正负类标签，且可并行处理多个正类，显著提升检测速度，尤其适合单目标检测。

item.title: 经典AI缩放定律的解释与效率驱动的未来
item.content:encoded:
经典AI缩放定律揭示了计算与训练损失的关系，强调效率提升对持续进步的重要性。



摘要： 本文探讨了经典AI缩放定律在预训练中的表现，指出其虽然具有预测性但本质上是经验性的。尽管存在边际收益递减，实践中仍通过效率提升维持进展。作者认为，这些定律之所以广泛适用，是因为它们抽象了实现细节，将计算视为与实现无关的逻辑计算。实际缩放的负担取决于资源转化为计算的效率。这种抽象解释了定律的跨场景适用性，并揭示了硬件、算法和系统层面的效率竞赛。一旦效率被明确，核心问题变为需要多少效率提升才能保持缩放的有效性，这不仅意味着损失曲线的平缓化，也带来了成本降低、系统创新和突破的需求。

item.title: Hydra：统一视觉-语言模型实现检索与生成
item.content:encoded:
Hydra通过单模型实现检索与生成，提升效率并减少内存占用。



摘要： 本文提出Hydra，一种基于单个视觉-语言模型（VLM）的双头方法，支持ColBERT风格的晚期交互检索和自回归生成。通过切换LoRA适配器，在保持生成质量的同时实现多向量嵌入。实验表明，Hydra在多个VQA基准上表现接近独立基线模型，且在ViDoRe数据集上表现出良好的聚合得分。该设计减少了41%的峰值GPU内存，但适配器切换带来一定吞吐量开销。此外，该方法还可扩展至音频和视频任务。

item.title: LLM代理权限安全评估：GrantBox框架的提出与实验分析
item.content:encoded:
LLM代理在真实工具使用中存在安全风险，GrantBox评估其权限滥用情况。



摘要： 本文探讨了将真实世界工具赋予大语言模型（LLM）代理后带来的安全挑战。尽管这种配置能提升生产力，但也增加了信息泄露和基础设施损坏的风险。现有的安全评估基准多依赖预设工具和受限交互模式，无法反映真实环境中的安全性。为此，作者提出了GrantBox，一个用于分析代理权限使用的安全评估沙盒。该框架自动集成真实工具，并允许代理调用实际权限，从而评估其在提示注入攻击下的表现。实验结果显示，虽然LLM具备一定的安全意识，但仍易受复杂攻击影响，平均攻击成功率高达84.80%。

item.title: 基于FLUX的旧照片色彩还原方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新框架，提升旧照片色彩还原效果。



摘要： 本文针对旧照片色彩还原难题，提出基于生成扩散模型FLUX的新框架。该方法采用结构-色彩解耦策略，分离结构保留与色彩修复，实现更准确的色彩还原。同时引入渐进式直接偏好优化策略，提升模型对色彩偏好的学习能力，并通过视觉语义提示提取照片中的细粒度语义信息，减少色彩偏差。实验表明，该方法在合成与真实数据集上均优于现有先进方法，包括商业模型，可生成高质量、生动的色彩化结果。

item.title: DreamLite: A Lightweight On-Device Unified Model for Image Generation and Editing
item.content:encoded: Diffusion models have made significant progress in both text-to-image (T2I) generation and text-guided image editing. However, these models are typically built with billions of parameters, leading to high latency and increased deployment challenges. While on-device diffusion models improve efficiency, they largely focus on T2I generation and lack support for image editing. In this paper, we propose DreamLite, a compact unified on-device diffusion model (0.39B) that supports both T2I generation and text-guided image editing within a single network. DreamLite is built on a pruned mobile U-Net backbone and unifies conditioning through in-context spatial concatenation in the latent space. It concatenates images horizontally as input, using a (target | blank) configuration for generation tasks and (target | source) for editing tasks. To stabilize the training of this compact model, we introduce a task-progressive joint pretraining strategy that sequentially targets T2I, editing, and joint tasks. After high-quality SFT and reinforcement learning, DreamLite achieves GenEval (0.72) for image generation and ImgEdit (4.11) for image editing, outperforming existing on-device models and remaining competitive with several server-side models. By employing step distillation, we further reduce denoising processing to just 4 steps, enabling our DreamLite could generate or edit a 1024 x 1024 image in less than 1s on a Xiaomi 14 smartphone. To the best of our knowledge, DreamLite is the first unified on-device diffusion model that supports both image generation and image editing.

item.title: NeiGAD: Augmenting Graph Anomaly Detection via Spectral Neighbor Information
item.content:encoded: Graph anomaly detection (GAD) aims to identify irregular nodes or structures in attributed graphs. Neighbor information, which reflects both structural connectivity and attribute consistency with surrounding nodes, is essential for distinguishing anomalies from normal patterns. Although recent graph neural network (GNN)-based methods incorporate such information through message passing, they often fail to explicitly model its effect or interaction with attributes, limiting detection performance. This work introduces NeiGAD, a novel plug-and-play module that captures neighbor information through spectral graph analysis. Theoretical insights demonstrate that eigenvectors of the adjacency matrix encode local neighbor interactions and progressively amplify anomaly signals. Based on this, NeiGAD selects a compact set of eigenvectors to construct efficient and discriminative representations. Experiments on eight real-world datasets show that NeiGAD consistently improves detection accuracy and outperforms state-of-the-art GAD methods. These results demonstrate the importance of explicit neighbor modeling and the effectiveness of spectral analysis in anomaly detection. Code is available at: https://github.com/huafeihuang/NeiGAD.

item.title: Compressing Transformer Language Models via Matrix Product Operator Decomposition: A Case Study on PicoGPT
item.content:encoded: Transformer-based language models achieve strong performance across NLP tasks, but their quadratic parameter scaling with hidden dimension makes deployment on resource-constrained hardware expensive. We study Matrix Product Operator (MPO) decomposition as a principled compression method for transformers. MPO factorises weight matrices into chains of low-rank cores, with approximation quality controlled by the bond dimension chi. We replace every nn.Linear layer in PicoGPT, a GPT-2-style character-level language model with about 1M parameters, with an MPOLinear module parameterised as an MPO chain. Cores are initialised either by TT-SVD from pretrained dense weights or from random initialisation, and trained using standard PyTorch autograd without a custom backward pass. We derive balanced factorisation schemes for the five distinct weight shapes in PicoGPT and evaluate bond dimensions chi in {4, 8, 16, 32} on Tiny Shakespeare. MPO compression achieves up to 13x compression per transformer block at chi = 4. At chi = 16, the model uses 191,872 parameters instead of 1,020,224 while retaining 97.7% of baseline token accuracy (51.6% vs 52.8%). Reconstruction error follows the expected trend and is lower for three-site than two-site factorisations at the same bond dimension. The chi = 8 model gives the best accuracy per parameter, exceeding the dense baseline by 2.7x on this metric. These results show that MPO parameterisation is a practical and theoretically grounded alternative to low-rank methods and unstructured pruning for transformer compression.

item.title: TerraSky3D: Multi-View Reconstructions of European Landmarks in 4K
item.content:encoded: Despite the growing need for data of more and more sophisticated 3D reconstruction pipelines, we can still observe a scarcity of suitable public datasets. Existing 3D datasets are either low resolution, limited to a small amount of scenes, based on images of varying quality because retrieved from the internet, or limited to specific capturing scenarios. Motivated by this lack of suitable 3D datasets, we captured TerraSky3D, a high-resolution large-scale 3D reconstruction dataset comprising 50,000 images divided into 150 ground, aerial, and mixed scenes. The dataset focuses on European landmarks and comes with curated calibration data, camera poses, and depth maps. TerraSky3D tries to answer the need for challenging dataset that can be used to train and evaluate 3D reconstruction-related pipelines.

item.title: LIBERO-Para: A Diagnostic Benchmark and Metrics for Paraphrase Robustness in VLA Models
item.content:encoded: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models achieve strong performance in robotic manipulation by leveraging pre-trained vision-language backbones. However, in downstream robotic settings, they are typically fine-tuned with limited data, leading to overfitting to specific instruction formulations and leaving robustness to paraphrased instructions underexplored. To study this gap, we introduce LIBERO-Para, a controlled benchmark that independently varies action expressions and object references for fine-grained analysis of linguistic generalization. Across seven VLA configurations (0.6B-7.5B), we observe consistent performance degradation of 22-52 pp under paraphrasing. This degradation is primarily driven by object-level lexical variation: even simple synonym substitutions cause large drops, indicating reliance on surface-level matching rather than semantic grounding. Moreover, 80-96% of failures arise from planning-level trajectory divergence rather than execution errors, showing that paraphrasing disrupts task identification. Binary success rate treats all paraphrases equally, obscuring whether models perform consistently across difficulty levels or rely on easier cases. To address this, we propose PRIDE, a metric that quantifies paraphrase difficulty using semantic and syntactic factors. Our benchmark and corresponding code are available at: https://github.com/cau-hai-lab/LIBERO-Para

item.title: Efficient Inference of Large Vision Language Models
item.content:encoded: Although Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) have demonstrated impressive multimodal reasoning capabilities, their scalability and deployment are constrained by massive computational requirements. In particular, the massive amount of visual tokens from high-resolution input data aggravates the situation due to the quadratic complexity of attention mechanisms. To address these issues, the research community has developed several optimization frameworks. This paper presents a comprehensive survey of the current state-of-the-art techniques for accelerating LVLM inference. We introduce a systematic taxonomy that categorizes existing optimization frameworks into four primary dimensions: visual token compression, memory management and serving, efficient architectural design, and advanced decoding strategies. Furthermore, we critically examine the limitations of these current methodologies and identify critical open problems to inspire future research directions in efficient multimodal systems.

item.title: GEMS: Agent-Native Multimodal Generation with Memory and Skills
item.content:encoded: Recent multimodal generation models have achieved remarkable progress on general-purpose generation tasks, yet continue to struggle with complex instructions and specialized downstream tasks. Inspired by the success of advanced agent frameworks such as Claude Code, we propose \textbf{GEMS} (Agent-Native Multimodal \textbf{GE}neration with \textbf{M}emory and \textbf{S}kills), a framework that pushes beyond the inherent limitations of foundational models on both general and downstream tasks. GEMS is built upon three core components. Agent Loop introduces a structured multi-agent framework that iteratively improves generation quality through closed-loop optimization. Agent Memory provides a persistent, trajectory-level memory that hierarchically stores both factual states and compressed experiential summaries, enabling a global view of the optimization process while reducing redundancy. Agent Skill offers an extensible collection of domain-specific expertise with on-demand loading, allowing the system to effectively handle diverse downstream applications. Across five mainstream tasks and four downstream tasks, evaluated on multiple generative backends, GEMS consistently achieves significant performance gains. Most notably, it enables the lightweight 6B model Z-Image-Turbo to surpass the state-of-the-art Nano Banana 2 on GenEval2, demonstrating the effectiveness of agent harness in extending model capabilities beyond their original limits.

item.title: FedDES: Graph-Based Dynamic Ensemble Selection for Personalized Federated Learning
item.content:encoded: Statistical heterogeneity in Federated Learning (FL) often leads to negative transfer, where a single global model fails to serve diverse client distributions. Personalized federated learning (pFL) aims to address this by tailoring models to individual clients. However, under most existing pFL approaches, clients integrate peer client contributions uniformly, which ignores the reality that not all peers are likely to be equally beneficial. Additionally, the potential for personalization at the instance level remains largely unexplored, even though the reliability of different peer models often varies across individual samples within the same client. We introduce FedDES (Federated Dynamic Ensemble Selection), a decentralized pFL framework that achieves instance-level personalization through dynamic ensemble selection. Central to our approach is a Graph Neural Network (GNN) meta-learner trained on a heterogeneous graph modeling interactions between data samples and candidate classifiers. For each test query, the GNN dynamically selects and weights peer client models, forming an ensemble of the most competent classifiers while effectively suppressing contributions from those that are irrelevant or potentially harmful for performance. Experiments on CIFAR-10 and real-world ICU healthcare data demonstrate that FedDES outperforms state-of-the-art pFL baselines in non-IID settings, offering robust protection against negative transfer.

item.title: DiffAttn: Diffusion-Based Drivers' Visual Attention Prediction with LLM-Enhanced Semantic Reasoning
item.content:encoded: Drivers' visual attention provides critical cues for anticipating latent hazards and directly shapes decision-making and control maneuvers, where its absence can compromise traffic safety. To emulate drivers' perception patterns and advance visual attention prediction for intelligent vehicles, we propose DiffAttn, a diffusion-based framework that formulates this task as a conditional diffusion-denoising process, enabling more accurate modeling of drivers' attention. To capture both local and global scene features, we adopt Swin Transformer as encoder and design a decoder that combines a Feature Fusion Pyramid for cross-layer interaction with dense, multi-scale conditional diffusion to jointly enhance denoising learning and model fine-grained local and global scene contexts. Additionally, a large language model (LLM) layer is incorporated to enhance top-down semantic reasoning and improve sensitivity to safety-critical cues. Extensive experiments on four public datasets demonstrate that DiffAttn achieves state-of-the-art (SoTA) performance, surpassing most video-based, top-down-feature-driven, and LLM-enhanced baselines. Our framework further supports interpretable driver-centric scene understanding and has the potential to improve in-cabin human-machine interaction, risk perception, and drivers' state measurement in intelligent vehicles.

item.title: Skillful Kilometer-Scale Regional Weather Forecasting via Global and Regional Coupling
item.content:encoded: Data-driven weather models have advanced global medium-range forecasting, yet high-resolution regional prediction remains challenging due to unresolved multiscale interactions between large-scale dynamics and small-scale processes such as terrain-induced circulations and coastal effects. This paper presents a global-regional coupling framework for kilometer-scale regional weather forecasting that synergistically couples a pretrained Transformer-based global model with a high-resolution regional network via a novel bidirectional coupling module, ScaleMixer. ScaleMixer dynamically identifies meteorologically critical regions through adaptive key-position sampling and enables cross-scale feature interaction through dedicated attention mechanisms. The framework produces forecasts at $0.05^\circ$ ($\sim 5 \mathrm{km}$ ) and 1-hour resolution over China, significantly outperforming operational NWP and AI baselines on both gridded reanalysis data and real-time weather station observations. It exhibits exceptional skill in capturing fine-grained phenomena such as orographic wind patterns and Foehn warming, demonstrating effective global-scale coherence with high-resolution fidelity. The code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/ScaleMixer-6B66.

item.title: PoseDreamer: Scalable and Photorealistic Human Data Generation Pipeline with Diffusion Models
item.content:encoded: Acquiring labeled datasets for 3D human mesh estimation is challenging due to depth ambiguities and the inherent difficulty of annotating 3D geometry from monocular images. Existing datasets are either real, with manually annotated 3D geometry and limited scale, or synthetic, rendered from 3D engines that provide precise labels but suffer from limited photorealism, low diversity, and high production costs. In this work, we explore a third path: generated data. We introduce PoseDreamer, a novel pipeline that leverages diffusion models to generate large-scale synthetic datasets with 3D mesh annotations. Our approach combines controllable image generation with Direct Preference Optimization for control alignment, curriculum-based hard sample mining, and multi-stage quality filtering. Together, these components naturally maintain correspondence between 3D labels and generated images, while prioritizing challenging samples to maximize dataset utility. Using PoseDreamer, we generate more than 500,000 high-quality synthetic samples, achieving a 76% improvement in image-quality metrics compared to rendering-based datasets. Models trained on PoseDreamer achieve performance comparable to or superior to those trained on real-world and traditional synthetic datasets. In addition, combining PoseDreamer with synthetic datasets results in better performance than combining real-world and synthetic datasets, demonstrating the complementary nature of our dataset. We will release the full dataset and generation code.

item.title: Heddle: A Distributed Orchestration System for Agentic RL Rollout
item.content:encoded: Agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) enables LLMs to solve complex tasks by alternating between a data-collection rollout phase and a policy training phase. During rollout, the agent generates trajectories, i.e., multi-step interactions between LLMs and external tools. Yet, frequent tool calls induce long-tailed trajectory generation that bottlenecks rollouts. This stems from step-centric designs that ignore trajectory context, triggering three system problems for long-tail trajectory generation: queueing delays, interference overhead, and inflated per-token time. We propose Heddle, a trajectory-centric system to optimize the when, where, and how of agentic rollout execution. Heddle integrates three core mechanisms: trajectory-level scheduling using runtime prediction and progressive priority to minimize cumulative queueing; trajectory-aware placement via presorted dynamic programming and opportunistic migration during idle tool call intervals to minimize interference; and trajectory-adaptive resource manager that dynamically tunes model parallelism to accelerate the per-token time of long-tail trajectories while maintaining high throughput for short trajectories. Evaluations across diverse agentic RL workloads demonstrate that Heddle effectively neutralizes the long-tail bottleneck, achieving up to 2.5$\times$ higher end-to-end rollout throughput compared to state-of-the-art baselines.

item.title: GeoHCC: Local Geometry-Aware Hierarchical Context Compression for 3D Gaussian Splatting
item.content:encoded: Although 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) enables high-fidelity real-time rendering, its prohibitive storage overhead severely hinders practical deployment. Recent anchor-based 3DGS compression schemes reduce redundancy through context modeling, yet overlook explicit geometric dependencies, leading to structural degradation and suboptimal rate-distortion performance. In this paper, we propose GeoHCC, a geometry-aware 3DGS compression framework that incorporates inter-anchor geometric correlations into anchor pruning and entropy coding for compact representation. We first introduce Neighborhood-Aware Anchor Pruning (NAAP), which evaluates anchor importance via weighted neighborhood feature aggregation and merges redundant anchors into salient neighbors, yielding a compact yet geometry-consistent anchor set. Building upon this optimized structure, we further develop a hierarchical entropy coding scheme, in which coarse-to-fine priors are exploited through a lightweight Geometry-Guided Convolution (GG-Conv) operator to enable spatially adaptive context modeling and rate-distortion optimization. Extensive experiments demonstrate that GeoHCC effectively resolves the structure preservation bottleneck, maintaining superior geometric integrity and rendering fidelity over state-of-the-art anchor-based approaches.

item.title: FedFG: Privacy-Preserving and Robust Federated Learning via Flow-Matching Generation
item.content:encoded: Federated learning (FL) enables distributed clients to collaboratively train a global model using local private data. Nevertheless, recent studies show that conventional FL algorithms still exhibit deficiencies in privacy protection, and the server lacks a reliable and stable aggregation rule for updating the global model. This situation creates opportunities for adversaries: on the one hand, they may eavesdrop on uploaded gradients or model parameters, potentially leaking benign clients' private data; on the other hand, they may compromise clients to launch poisoning attacks that corrupt the global model. To balance accuracy and security, we propose FedFG, a robust FL framework based on flow-matching generation that simultaneously preserves client privacy and resists sophisticated poisoning attacks. On the client side, each local network is decoupled into a private feature extractor and a public classifier. Each client is further equipped with a flow-matching generator that replaces the extractor when interacting with the server, thereby protecting private features while learning an approximation of the underlying data distribution. Complementing the client-side design, the server employs a client-update verification scheme and a novel robust aggregation mechanism driven by synthetic samples produced by the flow-matching generator. Experiments on MNIST, FMNIST, and CIFAR-10 demonstrate that, compared with prior work, our approach adapts to multiple attack strategies and achieves higher accuracy while maintaining strong privacy protection.

item.title: Attention Frequency Modulation: Training-Free Spectral Modulation of Diffusion Cross-Attention
item.content:encoded: Cross-attention is the primary interface through which text conditions latent diffusion models, yet its step-wise multi-resolution dynamics remain under-characterized, limiting principled training-free control. We cast diffusion cross-attention as a spatiotemporal signal on the latent grid by summarizing token-softmax weights into token-agnostic concentration maps and tracking their radially binned Fourier power over denoising. Across prompts and seeds, encoder cross-attention exhibits a consistent coarse-to-fine spectral progression, yielding a stable time-frequency fingerprint of token competition. Building on this structure, we introduce Attention Frequency Modulation (AFM), a plug-and-play inference-time intervention that edits token-wise pre-softmax cross-attention logits in the Fourier domain: low- and high-frequency bands are reweighted with a progress-aligned schedule and can be adaptively gated by token-allocation entropy, before the token softmax. AFM provides a continuous handle to bias the spatial scale of token-competition patterns without retraining, prompt editing, or parameter updates. Experiments on Stable Diffusion show that AFM reliably redistributes attention spectra and produces substantial visual edits while largely preserving semantic alignment. Finally, we find that entropy mainly acts as an adaptive gain on the same frequency-based edit rather than an independent control axis.

item.title: EarlySciRev: A Dataset of Early-Stage Scientific Revisions Extracted from LaTeX Writing Traces
item.content:encoded: Scientific writing is an iterative process that generates rich revision traces, yet publicly available resources typically expose only final or near-final versions of papers. This limits empirical study of revision behaviour and evaluation of large language models (LLMs) for scientific writing. We introduce EarlySciRev, a dataset of early-stage scientific text revisions automatically extracted from arXiv LaTeX source files. Our key observation is that commented-out text in LaTeX often preserves discarded or alternative formulations written by the authors themselves. By aligning commented segments with nearby final text, we extract paragraph-level candidate revision pairs and apply LLM-based filtering to retain genuine revisions. Starting from 1.28M candidate pairs, our pipeline yields 578k validated revision pairs, grounded in authentic early drafting traces. We additionally provide a human-annotated benchmark for revision detection. EarlySciRev complements existing resources focused on late-stage revisions or synthetic rewrites and supports research on scientific writing dynamics, revision modelling, and LLM-assisted editing.

item.title: Adapting SAM to Nuclei Instance Segmentation and Classification via Cooperative Fine-Grained Refinement
item.content:encoded: Nuclei instance segmentation is critical in computational pathology for cancer diagnosis and prognosis. Recently, the Segment Anything Model has demonstrated exceptional performance in various segmentation tasks, leveraging its rich priors and powerful global context modeling capabilities derived from large-scale pre-training on natural images. However, directly applying SAM to the medical imaging domain faces significant limitations: it lacks sufficient perception of the local structural features that are crucial for nuclei segmentation, and full fine-tuning for downstream tasks requires substantial computational costs. To efficiently transfer SAM's robust prior knowledge to nuclei instance segmentation while supplementing its task-aware local perception, we propose a parameter-efficient fine-tuning framework, named Cooperative Fine-Grained Refinement of SAM, consisting of three core components: 1) a Multi-scale Adaptive Local-aware Adapter, which enables effective capability transfer by augmenting the frozen SAM backbone with minimal parameters and instilling a powerful perception of local structures through dynamically generated, multi-scale convolutional kernels; 2) a Hierarchical Modulated Fusion Module, which dynamically aggregates multi-level encoder features to preserve fine-grained spatial details; and 3) a Boundary-Guided Mask Refinement, which integrates multi-context boundary cues with semantic features through explicit supervision, producing a boundary-focused signal to refine initial mask predictions for sharper delineation. These three components work cooperatively to enhance local perception, preserve spatial details, and refine boundaries, enabling SAM to perform accurate nuclei instance segmentation directly.

item.title: Membership Inference Attacks against Large Audio Language Models
item.content:encoded: We present the first systematic Membership Inference Attack (MIA) evaluation of Large Audio Language Models (LALMs). As audio encodes non-semantic information, it induces severe train and test distribution shifts and can lead to spurious MIA performance. Using a multi-modal blind baseline based on textual, spectral, and prosodic features, we demonstrate that common speech datasets exhibit near-perfect train/test separability (AUC approximately 1.0) even without model inference, and the standard MIA scores strongly correlate with these blind acoustic artifacts (correlation greater than 0.7). Using this blind baseline, we identify that distribution-matched datasets enable reliable MIA evaluation without distribution shift confounds. We benchmark multiple MIA methods and conduct modality disentanglement experiments on these datasets. The results reveal that LALM memorization is cross-modal, arising only from binding a speaker's vocal identity with its text. These findings establish a principled standard for auditing LALMs beyond spurious correlations.

item.title: Reducing Oracle Feedback with Vision-Language Embeddings for Preference-Based RL
item.content:encoded: Preference-based reinforcement learning can learn effective reward functions from comparisons, but its scalability is constrained by the high cost of oracle feedback. Lightweight vision-language embedding (VLE) models provide a cheaper alternative, but their noisy outputs limit their effectiveness as standalone reward generators. To address this challenge, we propose ROVED, a hybrid framework that combines VLE-based supervision with targeted oracle feedback. Our method uses the VLE to generate segment-level preferences and defers to an oracle only for samples with high uncertainty, identified through a filtering mechanism. In addition, we introduce a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that adapts the VLE with the obtained oracle feedback in order to improve the model over time in a synergistic fashion. This ensures the retention of the scalability of embeddings and the accuracy of oracles, while avoiding their inefficiencies. Across multiple robotic manipulation tasks, ROVED matches or surpasses prior preference-based methods while reducing oracle queries by up to 80%. Remarkably, the adapted VLE generalizes across tasks, yielding cumulative annotation savings of up to 90%, highlighting the practicality of combining scalable embeddings with precise oracle supervision for preference-based RL.

item.title: OptINC: Optical In-Network-Computing for Scalable Distributed Learning
item.content:encoded: Distributed learning is widely used for training large models on large datasets by distributing parts of the model or dataset across multiple devices and aggregating the computed results for subsequent computations or parameter updates. Existing communication algorithms for distributed learning such as ring all-reduce result in heavy communication overhead between servers. Since communication in large-scale systems uses optical fibers, we propose an Optical In-Network-Computing (OptINC) architecture to offload the computation in servers onto the optical interconnects. To execute gradient averaging and quantization in the optical domain, we incorporate optical devices such as Mach-Zehnder-Interferometers (MZIs) into the interconnects. Such a de facto optical neural network (ONN) can effectively reduce the communication overhead in existing distributed training solutions. To reduce dataset complexity for training this neural network, a preprocessing algorithm implemented in the optical domain is also proposed. Hardware cost is lowered by approximating the weight matrices of the optical neural network with unitary and diagonal matrices, while the accuracy is maintained by a proposed hardware-aware training algorithm. The proposed solution was evaluated on real distributed learning tasks, including ResNet50 on CIFAR-100, and a LLaMA-based network on Wikipedia-1B. In both cases, the proposed framework can achieve comparable training accuracy to the ring all-reduce baseline, while eliminating communication overhead.

item.title: 基于CHIRP数据集和CORVID方法的野生动物行为监测研究
item.content:encoded:
提出CHIRP数据集与CORVID方法，提升野生动物个体识别准确性。



摘要： 本文介绍了CHIRP数据集和CORVID方法，用于提升野生动物行为监测的准确性。CHIRP数据集涵盖多种计算机视觉任务，支持个体再识别、动作识别等。CORVID方法通过颜色腿环识别鸟类个体，并采用概率跟踪技术。研究还引入了生物相关指标评估模型性能，结果表明CORVID优于现有方法。该工作为连接计算机视觉与生物应用提供了参考。

item.title: 因果表示学习的有限样本保证
item.content:encoded:
在少量环境中学习因果表示的理论分析。



摘要： 本文研究了在子线性数量的环境中，如何从数据中学习因果表示。通过引入多节点干预，作者证明了即使干预目标未知，也能有效学习到潜在的因果图、混合矩阵和表示。研究提供了新的扰动分析方法，确保了因果表示的一致性恢复，为因果表示学习提供了理论支持。

item.title: FEAST：基于全连接注意力的新型空间转录组基因表达预测方法
item.content:encoded:
FEAST通过全连接图和负向注意机制提升空间转录组分析精度。



摘要： 本文提出FEAST，一种基于注意力机制的空间转录组分析框架。该方法采用全连接图结构，考虑所有点对之间的相互作用，克服了传统图神经网络依赖预定义稀疏图的局限性。同时引入负向注意机制，捕捉生物过程中的抑制关系，并通过非网格采样策略增强形态学上下文信息。实验表明，FEAST在基因表达预测任务中优于现有方法，并能生成具有生物学意义的注意力图。

item.title: 评估视觉语言模型在检测误导性可视化中的能力
item.content:encoded:
研究评估VLM在识别误导性图表中的表现，发现其对设计错误更敏感。



摘要： 本文研究了视觉语言模型（VLMs）在识别误导性数据可视化方面的能力，特别是当误导源于图表说明中的推理错误或设计问题时。作者构建了一个基准测试，结合真实图表和人工编写的误导性说明，以系统分析不同类型的误导行为。实验结果显示，VLMs在检测图表设计错误方面表现较好，但在识别基于推理的误导信息上存在不足，并且容易将非误导性图表误判为误导。该研究填补了现有技术在区分误导类型方面的空白。

item.title: 基于感知-探索策略优化的多模态思维链推理方法
item.content:encoded:
PEPO提升多模态思维链推理性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为感知-探索策略优化（PEPO）的方法，用于改进多模态思维链（CoT）推理。通过在token级别分析推理轨迹，发现成功的推理依赖于结构化的token动态，反映了感知基础和探索性推理。PEPO利用隐藏状态相似性构建感知先验，并结合token熵通过平滑门控机制生成token级优势，与现有RLVR框架如GRPO和DAPO兼容，无需额外监督或辅助分支。实验表明，PEPO在多个多模态基准任务中均取得显著提升，包括几何推理、视觉定位、视觉解谜和少样本分类，同时保持训练稳定性。

item.title: 人类如何通过经验调整对AI信心信号的依赖
item.content:encoded:
研究显示人类可通过经验学习调整对AI信心的判断。



摘要： 本研究探讨了人类是否能通过反复体验来重新校准对AI信心信号的依赖。在一项涉及200名参与者的实验中，参与者在四种不同的AI校准条件下预测AI的正确性，包括标准、过度自信、过度不自信以及反直觉的“逆向信心”映射。结果显示，所有条件下的参与者都表现出显著的学习能力，其准确性、区分度和校准一致性均有所提高。研究提出了一种基于线性对数几率变换和Rescorla-Wagner学习规则的计算模型，解释了人类如何通过更新基础信任水平和信心敏感性来适应AI系统。尽管人类可以弥补单调的错误校准，但在逆向信心情境中仍存在明显的学习边界，部分参与者难以克服初始的归纳偏见。

item.title: 基于元学习的单图像人体网格恢复方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种元学习框架提升单图像人体网格恢复效果。



摘要： 本文针对单图像人体网格恢复中的深度模糊和跨域泛化问题，提出一种结合元学习和自适应优化的方法。该方法通过模拟测试时优化过程来学习更优的参数初始化，并引入选择性参数缓存机制和基于分布的自适应更新策略，以减少计算开销并提高鲁棒性。实验表明，该方法在多个基准数据集上表现优异，显著降低了MPJPE指标，同时具备良好的跨域适应能力和不确定性估计。

item.title: 3D高斯点云的不确定性估计方法提升空间映射可靠性
item.content:encoded:
提出一种轻量级不确定性估计框架，提升3D高斯点云的空间映射可靠性。



摘要： 本文提出一种轻量、可插拔的像素级视图依赖性不确定性估计框架，用于3D高斯点云渲染。该方法通过贝叶斯正则化线性最小二乘优化重构残差，无需修改原始场景表示即可提取不确定性通道，从而提高空间映射的可靠性。实验表明，该方法在主动视角选择、无姿态场景变化检测和异常检测等任务中显著提升了性能。

item.title: 基于频域融合的降水临近预报方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出PW-FouCast框架提升降水预测精度与时效。



摘要： 降水临近预报对防灾减灾和航空安全至关重要。传统雷达模型在长时距预测中表现不佳，而现有融合气象变量的方法未能解决雷达图像与气象数据之间的表征差异。为此，本文提出PW-FouCast，一种基于傅里叶域的融合框架，利用Pangu-Weather预测作为频谱先验。该框架包含三个创新点：Pangu-Weather引导的频域调制、频域记忆模块以及逆向频域注意力机制，有效提升了高频率细节重建能力。实验结果表明，PW-FouCast在SEVIR和MeteoNet基准测试中达到最佳性能，显著延长了可靠预报时间。

item.title: ALADIN：基于细粒度属性对齐的轻量级ReID模型
item.content:encoded:
ALADIN通过细粒度属性对齐提升ReID性能。



摘要： 本文提出ALADIN，一种基于CLIP知识蒸馏的轻量级ReID模型。该方法引入细粒度属性局部对齐和场景感知提示生成器，以增强文本与视觉特征的自适应对应关系。通过属性局部蒸馏和跨模态对比与关系蒸馏，提升了模型在遮挡情况下的鲁棒性。同时利用多模态大模型生成结构化属性描述，并转换为局部注意力图进行监督。实验表明，ALADIN在Market-1501、DukeMTMC-reID和MSMT17数据集上优于传统CNN、Transformer和CLIP方法，具有更好的泛化性和可解释性。

item.title: AnyID：高保真身份保留视频生成框架
item.content:encoded:
AnyID实现高保真视频生成，支持多源身份输入与属性级控制。



摘要： 本文提出AnyID，一个高保真身份保留的视频生成框架，解决传统方法依赖单一身份参考的问题。该框架采用可扩展的全参考架构，统一多种身份输入（如人脸、肖像和视频）为一致表示，并引入主参考生成范式，通过差分提示实现属性级精确控制。训练过程中使用大规模高质量数据集，并通过强化学习进行微调，基于人类评估的偏好数据提升身份保真度和可控性。实验表明，AnyID在不同任务场景中均表现出卓越的身份保真度和属性控制能力。

item.title: 多目标遗传算法MOELIGA在特征选择中的应用
item.content:encoded:
MOELIGA有效解决特征选择中的精度与维度平衡问题。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为MOELIGA的多目标遗传算法，用于解决机器学习中的特征选择问题。该方法结合进化局部改进策略，通过拥挤适应度共享机制和S型变换提升多样性与紧凑性，并引入基于几何的目标以促进分类器独立性。在14个不同数据集上的实验表明，MOELIGA能够在保持或提升分类性能的同时，找到更小的特征子集，优于11种现有方法。研究结果表明，MOELIGA在处理高维复杂场景下的多目标特征选择问题上具有高效性和适应性。

item.title: 视频问答基准ForeSeaQA与AI取证搜索系统ForeSea
item.content:encoded:
提出ForeSeaQA基准和ForeSea系统提升视频多模态查询准确率。



摘要： 本文针对视频监控中目标定位困难的问题，提出了ForeSeaQA这一新型视频问答基准，支持图像与文本结合的多模态查询，并包含时间戳标注的关键事件。同时，作者设计了ForeSea AI取证搜索系统，包含跟踪、多模态嵌入和推理三个阶段，显著提升了视频检索和事件定位的准确性。实验表明，ForeSea在ForeSeaQA上比现有VideoRAG模型提高了3.5%的准确率和11.0%的时间IoU。ForeSeaQA是首个支持复杂多模态查询并具备精确时间定位能力的基准，ForeSea则是首个专为此场景设计的VideoRAG系统。

item.content:encoded:
物理信息神经网络提升多任务优化仿真效率与精度。



摘要： 文章探讨了在产品开发中，仿真与建模如何通过集成设计和制造过程以提高效率和质量。针对产品需求多样化带来的多任务优化挑战，提出了一种双物理信息神经网络架构，用于解耦参数化微分代数方程优化问题中的约束和目标函数。理论分析表明，引入带有全局误差界的压力变量可确保网络与优化问题的解等价性。结合遗传算法的训练框架提高了神经网络的训练精度和效率，避免了重复求解微分代数方程，实现了多任务目标的通用化，并保持对产品需求的实时响应能力。

item.title: 基于强化学习的语义类别自适应调度方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种自适应语义类别调度方法提升无监督域适应性能。



摘要： 文章探讨了语义类别学习顺序对无监督域适应的影响，特别是在恶劣天气条件下的语义分割任务中。传统方法依赖人工设计的启发式规则，难以适应模型训练过程中的动态变化。本文受强化学习启发，将课程学习建模为序列决策问题，提出一个自主的类别调度器，包含状态编码器和公平策略梯度目标，以实现类别的均衡提升。结合源域与目标域的混合监督，该方法能动态聚焦于最具信息量的类别，显著提升模型性能，并在多个基准数据集上取得最佳结果，展现出良好的泛化能力。

item.title: HEAR：高效音频表示模型提升资源受限设备性能
item.content:encoded:
HEAR通过解耦架构实现高效音频建模，参数与计算成本显著降低。



摘要： 本文提出HEAR（Human-inspired Efficient Audio Representation），一种基于人类认知机制的高效音频表示方法。该模型将处理流程分为局部特征提取和全局语义整合两个模块，并结合知识蒸馏训练的声学分词器，实现高效的掩码音频建模（MAM）。实验表明，HEAR仅需15M参数和9.47 GFLOPs即可达到与传统基础模型相当的性能，显著降低了计算成本，适用于资源受限设备。

item.title: 神经网络中规则与事实学习的理论模型研究
item.content:encoded:
提出RAF模型解析神经网络同时学习规则和记忆事实的能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Rules-and-Facts (RAF) 的最小可解模型，用于精确分析现代神经网络在学习规则和记忆事实方面的双重能力。该模型通过将训练标签分为由结构化规则生成的部分和随机标签的非结构化事实部分，结合统计物理中的教师-学生框架和Gardner容量分析，揭示了神经网络如何同时实现规则学习和记忆非结构化数据。研究结果表明，过参数化有助于同时实现这两个目标，而正则化和核函数或非线性选择则控制着能力分配。该模型为理解神经网络在推理结构的同时存储罕见或不可压缩信息提供了理论基础。

item.title: FeatDistill：一种高效的AI生成图像检测框架
item.content:encoded:
FeatDistill提升AI生成图像检测的鲁棒性与泛化能力。



摘要： 本文提出FeatDistill，一种结合特征蒸馏与多专家集成的AI生成图像检测框架，旨在解决真实场景中图像伪造检测的三大挑战：退化干扰、特征表示不足和泛化能力有限。通过构建由四个ViT骨干网络组成的集成模型，结合CLIP和SigLIP变体，捕捉互补的取证线索。训练过程中引入全面的退化建模和两阶段训练策略，提升模型的鲁棒性和语义一致性。推理时通过对四个独立训练专家的概率平均获得稳定决策，实验证明其在NTIRE挑战中表现出色，具备高效且实用的特性。

item.title: 基于交通理论的深度学习框架用于长期交通建模
item.content:encoded:
DeepDemand框架提升交通预测准确性与可解释性。



摘要： 本文提出DeepDemand，一个融合交通需求理论的深度学习框架，用于预测长期高速公路交通量。该框架结合了竞争性双源Dijkstra算法和可微架构，以提取出行需求区域并建模出行交互与时间衰减效应。在英国战略公路网络的8年数据上评估，DeepDemand在随机交叉验证中达到R²=0.718和MAE=7406辆，优于传统模型。其在空间交叉验证中也表现良好，显示良好的地理迁移能力。分析表明，该模型揭示了稳定的非线性时间衰减模式、关键经济驱动因素及多中心出行结构，为交通规划提供了可解释且实用的工具。

item.title: 深度神经网络的凸性与信号处理的结合
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示了ReLU网络的凸性，推动深度学习与信号处理的融合。



摘要： 本文探讨了使用ReLU激活函数的深度神经网络（DNN）在优化和理论理解上的挑战，并指出近年来发现的ReLU网络的凸性可以为训练和理解提供新思路。研究表明，某些神经网络架构，如两层ReLU网络及其他更深层或不同结构的网络，其损失函数具有隐藏的凸性。文章旨在通过连接深度学习的数学进展与传统信号处理方法，促进两者在实际应用中的结合与发展。

item.title: 基于状态保持的智能EDA自动化框架FluxEDA
item.content:encoded:
FluxEDA支持EDA工具的迭代优化与状态维护。



摘要： 本文提出FluxEDA，一个统一且具有状态保持能力的智能EDA基础设施。该框架通过管理网关接口和结构化请求响应处理，实现对异构EDA工具的持续交互，而非孤立调用。实验表明，FluxEDA可支持多步骤分析与优化，包括状态重用、回滚和协同迭代执行，为智能EDA自动化提供可靠基础。

item.title: 结构感知的RAG系统在生物医学文献中的评估与优化
item.content:encoded:
结构感知RAG提升多部分证据检索，改进生成质量。



摘要： 本文探讨了传统基于排名指标（如MRR）的生物医学RAG系统评估方法的局限性，指出其过于关注检索精度而忽视了检索广度。作者提出GraLC-RAG框架，结合结构感知的分块检测、UMLS知识图谱融合和图引导混合检索，提升多部分证据的检索能力。实验表明，结构感知方法可检索到更多文档结构部分，尽管在标准指标上表现稍逊，但在生成质量上显著提升。研究揭示了现有评估指标对结构检索的低估问题，并指出多部分信息整合是当前生物医学RAG的关键挑战。

item.title: RelayS2S：一种兼顾低延迟与响应质量的混合对话系统架构
item.content:encoded:
RelayS2S实现低延迟与高质量响应的平衡。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为RelayS2S的混合架构，旨在解决实时语音对话系统中延迟与响应质量之间的矛盾。该架构在检测到用户发言时并行运行两条路径：快速路径使用双工语音到语音模型生成短响应前缀以实现低延迟音频输出，而慢速路径则通过ASR-LLM级联模型生成高质量响应。一个轻量级学习验证器控制路径切换，确保在适当情况下提交前缀或回退到慢速路径。实验表明，RelayS2S在保持接近99%级联响应质量的同时，实现了与S2S模型相当的P90启动延迟，且随着慢速路径模型规模增大，优势更加明显。

item.title: SatGeo-NeRF：基于几何正则化的卫星影像重建方法
item.content:encoded:
SatGeo-NeRF通过几何正则化提升卫星影像重建精度。



摘要： 本文提出SatGeo-NeRF，一种基于几何正则化的神经辐射场（NeRF）方法，用于解决现有模型在卫星影像重建中出现的过拟合导致的几何伪影问题。该方法引入三种模型无关的正则化策略：重力对齐平面正则化、粒度正则化和深度监督正则化，分别用于促进局部平面性、实现粗到细的几何学习以及稳定早期训练。在DFC2019卫星重建基准测试中，SatGeo-NeRF相比EO-NeRF和EO-GS等先进基线模型，在平均高度误差上分别提升了13.9%和11.7%。

item.title: 伊斯兰知识大语言模型基准测试与评估
item.content:encoded:
介绍伊斯兰MMLU基准，评估大语言模型在伊斯兰核心领域的表现。



摘要： 文章介绍了IslamicMMLU，这是一个包含10,013道多选题的基准测试，涵盖《古兰经》、圣训和教法三个领域。该基准用于评估大语言模型在伊斯兰知识方面的表现，测试结果显示不同模型在各领域的准确率差异较大，其中教法部分还引入了教法学派偏见检测任务。阿拉伯语专用模型表现不一，但整体仍落后于前沿模型。评估代码和排行榜已公开。

item.title: 生成式AI在教育中的用户体验：人机知识合作理论的构建
item.content:encoded:
生成式AI在教育中引发新的用户体验，需用新理论解释其知识合作特性。



摘要： 本文探讨了生成式AI（如ChatGPT）在教育中的应用对用户体验的影响。传统理论已无法充分解释GenAI带来的新型互动模式，如协商权威、认知重构和责任张力。为此，作者提出人类-人工智能知识合作理论（HAEPT），将用户与GenAI的关系视为一种动态的知识合作，涉及知识、代理和责任三方面的契约协商。研究指出，信任、依赖、学术诚信等问题应被理解为契约内部的张力，而非孤立现象。通过分析AI辅助学习和科学论证的案例，展示了不同契约配置下的合作模式，验证了HAEPT的有效性。

item.title: EngineAD：用于汽车领域异常检测的多变量真实数据集
item.content:encoded:
提出EngineAD数据集以促进汽车领域异常检测研究。



摘要： 本文介绍了EngineAD，一个包含25辆商用汽车在六个月内采集的高分辨率传感器数据的多变量数据集。该数据集具有真实操作数据和专家标注，能够区分正常状态与早期发动机故障迹象。通过将数据预处理为300时间步长的8个主成分，并使用九种不同的单类异常检测模型进行基准测试，研究发现不同车辆间的性能差异显著，且传统方法在某些情况下优于深度学习方法。作者希望通过公开发布EngineAD，为汽车行业的异常检测和预测提供现实且具有挑战性的资源。

item.title: 基于3D高斯的多模态场景预测框架Gau-Occ
item.content:encoded:
Gau-Occ通过3D高斯模型实现高效语义占用预测。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Gau-Occ的多模态场景预测框架，旨在提升自动驾驶中的3D语义占用预测性能。该框架通过将场景建模为紧凑的语义3D高斯集合，避免了传统方法中计算密集的体素或BEV张量处理。研究引入了LiDAR Completion Diffuser（LCD）以恢复稀疏LiDAR数据中的缺失结构，并通过Gaussian Anchor Fusion（GAF）实现多视角图像语义的高效融合。实验表明，Gau-Occ在多个基准测试中表现优异，同时具有显著的计算效率优势。
